rows.title: Destination Highlight: Tatoosh Islands Wilderness Kayak Adventure
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 8.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 270.0 lbs ?
Are you less than or equal to 78.0 inches ?
rows.description: Join our experienced kayak guide for a thrilling sea kayak paddle adventure in Tongass National Forest. Your 4.5-hour sea kayak journey begins with a motorized boat ride to Tatoosh Islands. On a protected beach, you’ll learn about kayak safety. Then, launch from the beach in a stable, double sea kayak. Paddle among the islands and learn about the natural wonders of a temperate rainforest environment. Keep on the lookout for intertidal life, including bald eagles, seals, minks, deer, porpoise, sea lions, and whales.
highlights: Launch in your double see kayak to see a variety of plant and animal life in the Tongass National Forest.
Float over to the Tatoosh Islands on a scenic inflatable boat ride while learning about kayak safety.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Big Nugget
rows.description: Enter the Gold Rush Training Camp, to learn how to extract paydirt, sluice, and assay your results. Learn the art of gold panning with a humorous and educational show. Practice your technique in heated troughs. Authentic gold is guaranteed! Weigh your fortune with our assayers. Meet canine athletes that have run the world-famous Iditarod race as well as our experienced mushers. Hear about the history of this breed through personal stories and an amazing collection of the gear necessary on long-distance races like the Iditarod. Observe the musher hooking them up to an authentic sled for a fast practice lap around the track. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed! Take a guided tour of the 350-ton Sixty Mile Dredge – one of the last rescued authentic gold dredges used for placer mining. Take a break with lunch at the Alaskan BBQ where our menu is based on Alaskan home-grown foods and finish with a campfire complete with s'mores, or soda floats (weather dependent), lots of performances, and laughter with our costumed characters. You will return to the ship not as a cheechako (newcomer to Alaska) but as a seasoned sourdough (someone who has experienced Alaska and is now a part of its history and culture) with lots of memories to share back home.
highlights: Meet Iditarod dogs and their mushers, and learn about the role sled dogs played in Alaska’s history. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed!
Visit the gold dredge and uncover its inner workings.
Savor a BBQ buffet, enjoying smoked ribs, chicken, salmon chowder delicious sides and dessert on the riverside patios.
Enjoy a campfire complete with s'mores or soda floats (weather dependent), lots of performances, and laughter with our costumed characters.
Become a prospector of the Gold Rush era and learn various gold panning techniques and search for authentic Yukon gold at the warm-water troughs, where valuable gold is guaranteed!

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Best of Skagway
rows.description: Experience many of Skagway's highlights in one tour. First, climb aboard the White Pass and Yukon Railway and ascend into the scenic mountains, hearing stories of the miners who made this difficult, or once-treacherous, sojourn on foot more than a century ago. Arriving in Fraser, British Columbia board a motor coach to travel south through the pass where your driver/guide will point out sites including Tormented Valley and Pitchfork Falls. At the foot of the pass is Liarsville Gold Rush Trail Camp. Here a cast of sourdoughs and dance hall girls will entertain you with a humorous melodrama, recite a poem by the "Bard of the North" and teach you the art of finding gold! You are guaranteed to hit pay dirt and you get to keep the gold you find... and there is nothing like panning for gold to work up an appetite! At the Liarsville Cookhouse the grill is fired up for a delicious feast of wild Alaskan salmon, baked chicken, rice pilaf, a variety of salads, baked beans, cornbread and dessert. Your tour culminates at the world-famous Red Onion Saloon where you will be greeted by sumptuously dressed madams. Upstairs in the Bombay room, enjoy a champagne toast to the courageous ladies of 1898. A few more steps takes you to what was the most luxurious brothel of its day, now faithfully restored to its present glory. On your tour through the brothel museum, you will hear stories of the infamous madams who worked there, and see the prized silver dress and other artifacts left behind by the "soiled doves". Embrace the spirit of '98 and enjoy the experience of a lifetime!
highlights: Travel the White Pass and Yukon Railway, following the footsteps of miners who travelled into the mountains on foot.
Discover the incredible beauty of British Columbia's Tormented Valley and Pitchfork Falls.
Sip a glass of champagne at the famous Red Onion Saloon.
Savor a delicious meal of wild Alaskan salmon, baked chicken, rice pilaf, salads, baked beans, cornbread, and dessert.
Immerse yourself in gold rush life at the Gold Rush Trail Camp where you'll learn how to pan for gold.

rows.title: Taku Lodge Feast and 5-Glacier Seaplane Discovery
restrictions: Are you less than or equal to 300.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Embark on an excursion, that takes to the air on a flightseeing tour and touches down for an authentic Alaskan feast. Climb into a seaplane and skid across the water before lifting off into the skies above Juneau. Every guest enjoys a window seat as you fly over five of Alaska’s most spectacular glaciers. You’ll also soar above the wilderness where you might spot black bear and other wildlife, commentary through your headset. Your flight concludes with an exhilarating water landing at Taku Glacier Lodge, the only place in Juneau where you can dine to majestic glacial views. Savor a feast of fresh Wild Alaskan Salmon (or chicken, if preferred) paired with a selection of side dishes made from the bounty of Alaska.
highlights: Gaze out of our window seat as your seaplane pilot flies over five spectacular glaciers.
Enjoy an exhilarating water landing atop the calm waters of the Taku River.
Savor a feast of fresh Wild Alaskan Salmon and scrumptious side dishes overlooking the glaciers.

restrictions: Are you less than or equal to 300.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Get ready to experience Juneau like you never imagined - high above it! Your epic glacier adventure begins the moment you board your seaplane. Feel your heart race as you take off and soar over the Tongass National Forest, your segway into a birds eye view of five unique glaciers. The ,500 square mile Juneau Icefield will leave you breathless as you explore deep mountain crevasses, crystal-blue meltwater pools and the mystic icecaps of these majestic wonders.

Explore Juneau's most majestic glaciers.
Guests enjoy a window seat and a headset for continuous narration.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: City Tour of Scenic Victoria
rows.description: Embark on a leisurely sightseeing drive that provides a captivating look at Victoria, while you relax in complete comfort. Your narrated exploration begins with a drive through extraordinary residential neighborhoods, where grand estates with manicured gardens overlook the Strait of San Juan de Fuca and the soaring Olympic Mountains. You'll witness Beacon Hill, the city's sprawling park located on the shores of the strait. The tour concludes with a drive along the Inner Harbor, the very heartbeat of Victoria. Here, you'll have the chance to debark at the historic Fairmont Empress Hotel and explore the Inner Harbor independently. Or, if you prefer, continue back to the ship. This tour is provided by an operator that has been third-party certified to meet the Global Sustainable Tourism Council's Criteria for Tour Operators.
highlights: Discover the beauty and history of Victoria, known as one of the most picturesque cities in North America.
Visit the Inner Harbor, the lively and historic centerpiece of the city.
See the Legislative Building, the Empress Hotel, the Royal British Columbia Museum and other sites.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Butchart Gardens and Taste of Tim Hortons
rows.description: Discover the natural splendor and historic charm of Canada, as you stop by the country’s iconic coffeehouse, followed by a visit to the Butchart Gardens - an enchanting masterpiece of colors, fragrances and floral displays.
highlights: Board your air-conditioned motor coach and set off on your picturesque Canadian journey, as your knowledgeable driver regales you with insightful commentary along the way. Make a brief stop at the iconic Tim Hortons, a beloved coffee and donut shop, where you can indulge in a couple of treats from this local favorite.
After, a scenic drive brings you to Butchart Gardens, a once barren space transformed into a 55-acre, century-old haven of beauty, adorned with lush greens and vibrant blooms.
Here, enjoy free time to explore at your own pace, as you stroll through the manicured pathways and admire the delicate details of each display.
Immerse yourself in the tranquility of these ethereal landscapes, before pausing at the garden’s gift shop for an unforgettable souvenir and departing with a complimentary memento (a Canada magnet).
As you return to your awaiting ship, pass by the highlights of downtown Victoria, including the Parliament Building, the Fairmont Empress Hotel and the sparkling waters of the city’s inner harbor.

rows.title: Small Group: Exclusive Flightseeing and Taste of Alaska
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 2.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Indulge in the perfect blend of Ketchikan's finest offerings, as you embark on an unforgettable journey combining spectacular scenery and mountain top flightseeing with a luxurious and authentic Alaskan seafood feast at a secluded world-famous George Inlet Lodge.
highlights: Discover firsthand why Alaska is affectionately known as “The Last Frontier." This extraordinary excursion promises a once-in-a-lifetime experience that will undoubtedly be the highlight of your Alaskan getaway.
Feel the exhilaration as a DeHavilland Beaver aircraft roars to life, with your skilled pilot at the helm, as you ascend from the sparkling ocean below.
Enjoy your pilot’s narration and details of the area with individual headsets and a guaranteed window seat.
Glide gently over the Tongass Narrows, taking in the bustling communities below and marveling at the awe-inspiring mountain ranges that define this remarkable corner of the world.

Your flight to George Inlet Lodge will transport you over rugged peaks at the southern tip of Revillagigedo Island. En route, keen observers might spot mountain goats resting on pristine snowpack or black bears foraging for succulent berries, or humpback whales breaching in the ocean.

As your floatplane gracefully touches down on the tranquil waters of George Inlet, you will arrive at the lodge where your host welcomes you. Have your cameras ready to capture the breathtaking sight of your floatplane taking off, framed by the magnificent Tongass National Forest.

Indulge in a mouthwatering Alaskan Tasting Adventure featuring your choice of three craft beers or Ciders from renowned Alaska breweries and Cidery, each carefully paired with a selection of delectable dishes.

Start with our homemade George Inlet Lodge crab chowder (or opt for a fresh salad with an Asian-style dressing). Savor the flavors of our fresh crab egg rolls and a crab cake made according to our lodge recipe, followed by a half steaming hot Dungeness crab, cooked to perfection.

Conclude your meal with a delightful piece of blueberry-topped cheesecake, a perfect blend of sweetness and tartness. As you savor each bite, you'll be entertained with a lesson in the art of crab cracking, hear entertaining stories about Alaskan Fishing and Crabbing as well as a brief history of the lodge.

Conclude your tour experience with a beautiful 25-minute coastal drive that provides a glimpse into our vibrant waterfront community and its local history, charming harbors, fascinating totem parks, and the authentic Alaskan way of life.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Butchart and Butterfly Gardens
rows.description: Spend your day surrounded by vivid and vibrant color with a tour to two of Victoria’s most popular attractions; Butchart and Butterfly Gardens. Meander along trails that lead you through an incredible 55-acres of landscaped perfection at Butchart Gardens. See the Rose Gardens, Italian Gardens, Japanese Gardens and legendary Sunken Gardens. Stroll beneath flittering wings of color at Butterfly Garden. You’ll see pink flamingoes and tropical birds, as well. You might even see a butterfly sleeping! This tour is provided by an operator that has been third-party certified to meet the Global Sustainable Tourism Council's Criteria for Tour Operators.
highlights: Explore one of the world's foremost botanical gardens on a self-guided tour.
Visit the famous Sunken Garden, an abandoned limestone quarry that was magically transformed into a masterpiece.
Explore the 12,000-square-foot Butterfly Garden and wander among a multitude of free-flying butterflies.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Railroad and Yukon Suspension Bridge Discovery
rows.description: Delight in Skagway's fascinating history and spectacular mountainous scenery as you travel to the White Pass Summit and the Yukon Suspension Bridge. Ride a restored vintage rail car on the White Pass & Yukon Route Railway, "The Scenic Railway of the World", as you delight to tales of the Klondike Gold Rush. Next, arrive at Fraser, British Columbia, and ride in the comfort of a motor coach across the White Pass summit to Tutshi Canyon where you can traverse the Yukon Suspension Bridge and enjoy the breathtaking view.
highlights: Admire breathtaking wilderness scenery while on an unforgettable journey on the White Pass & Yukon Route Railway, a narrow-gauge railway chiseled into a mountain during the Gold Rush.
Feel the adrenaline rush of crossing the Yukon Suspension Bridge, dangling 57 feet above a river roiling with class III and IV rapids.
Delve into the fascinating history of the region relayed by your informative guide, and stop for photos along the way at places such as Tormented Valley and Pitchfork Falls.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Enchanting Butchart Gardens
rows.description: Explore Victoria, renowned as Canada’s “City of Gardens” on this scenic drive through the historic downtown and visit to one of the world’s most recognized gardens—the breathtaking Butchart Gardens. Victoria’s gardens bloom year-round. Enjoy a scenic drive through the historic downtown quarter, viewing Victoria’s various districts as well as the Parliament Buildings and the iconic Empress Hotel. Then, travel to the enchanting Butchart Gardens, known as one of the best show gardens in the world. Stroll the Sunken Garden, the Rose Garden, the Japanese Garden, Star Pond, the Italian Garden, and the Mediterranean Garden. Learn about the garden’s diverse plant species at the Plant Identification Centre. Children can enjoy the Children’s Pavilion with its carousel. Butchart Gardens is a family-owned and operated National Historic Site, featuring over a million budding plants that bloom from March through October. Butchart Gardens has been celebrated by National Geographic, CNN, and Condé Nast. This tour is provided by an operator that has been third-party certified to meet the Global Sustainable Tourism Council's Criteria for Tour Operators.
highlights: Learn how the Butchart family transformed a barren rock quarry into one of the world's top horticulture attractions.
Enjoy a self-guided tour through this captivating and colorful botanical wonderland spanning 55 acres.
Take a drive through Chinatown and Victoria's historic Inner Harbor.

rows.title: Small Group: Skagway Glacier Discovery by Helicopter
restrictions: Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Leave civilization behind as you embark on an unforgettable journey to Alaska's remote glaciers. Begin your adventure at the historic Skagway waterfront, where you'll be outfitted with overboots before boarding a helicopter. Once you lift off, the magic begins. Fly across regal mountains, majestic valleys and colossal glaciers before landing on a magnificent glacier. Once you land, you'll have the opportunity to explore one of nature's greatest marvels on foot.
highlights: Experience what it feels like to stand on a glacier and walk across a river of ice.
Enjoy a bird's-eye-view of Skagway's breathtaking landscape as your helicopter soars through the sky.
See Mother Nature's best creations including the Chilkat Glacier, Ferebee Glacier, or Meade Glacier.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: UTV Safari, Cruise, Craft Beer and Dungeness Crab Meal
restrictions: Are you between 8.0 and 99.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 300.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Rev things up as you jump behind the wheel of a UTV on a thrilling, off-road safari through Alaska’s pristine scenery, followed by a scenic boat ride to George Inlet Lodge for a decadent crab lunch and craft beer tasting.
highlights: Take in the vibrant, small-town scenery and rich history of frontier town Ketchikan on your way to the UTV Base Camp.

Hop aboard your UTV and zoom out through the mountainous scenery of George Inlet Fjords, up old logging roads and across jaw-dropping bridges while marveling at the magnificent scenery of Mahoney Glacial Cirque.
Next, board a 46-foot, enclosed catamaran sightseeing vessel for a scenic seven-mile ride along the coast, with the Mahoney Glacial Cirque prominent miles below the trails you were just driving on.

highlights: Venture into the Thomas Basin historic district, Ketchikan’s charming downtown. Pass by Spruce Mill – once the largest Sitka Spruce Mill in the world and now home to the Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show arena.

rows.title: Small Group: Scenic River Float through Bald Eagle Preserve
rows.description: Discover the natural wonders and majestic wildlife of Alaska on a rafting exploration that glides you through the Chilkat Bald Eagle Preserve. Your expedition begins aboard a jet-powered catamaran that will take you through the waterways of Lynn Fjord to the town of Haines. A short, but panoramic, drive down the National Scenic Byway leads to the launch point for your river rafting journey. After a quick safety briefing, climb into an 18-foot inflatable raft and set off down the Chilkat River. Glide through the protected wilderness and keep watch for the park's famed birds of prey soaring overhead. You might also spot moose, or even bear roaming the riverbanks. Go ashore for a simple lunch and homemade cookies enjoyed against the most majestic backdrop imaginable. Follow the river’s edge back to Haines before the brisk catamaran ride back to Skagway.
highlights: Witness the majesty of Alaska on an expedition that travels by land, by sea, and by river.
Board a jet-powered catamaran and cruise through Lynn Fjord to the town of Haines, Alaska.
Board an 18-foot rubber raft for a guided journey down the Chilkat River and into its Bald Eagle preserve.

rows.title: Small Group: Luxury Whale Watch
rows.description: Take the trip of a lifetime on an exclusive, luxury whale watching experience where you're one of only eight guests onboard an exclusive luxury yacht led by an experienced naturalist and professional captain that offers premium Alaskan refreshments as you scan the beautiful ocean wilderness for the spectacular leviathans that call it home.
highlights: Meet your Mercedes Sprinter "land yacht" at the pier for a scenic, 25-minute drive to Auke Bay.

Board Alaska's only whale-watching yachts to set out on your adventure in style, including local appetizers and drinks, including Alaskan Brewery beer, wine, Champagne, soft drinks, coffee, and a scrumptious charcuterie board or breakfast board for morning trips.

Cast your eyes to the gorgeous and frigid waters during one of Alaska's best whale watching experiences to look for massive humpback whales thriving in their summer habitat. Orcas, bald eagles, harbor seals, sea lions, and porpoises join the list of amazing creatures you may see on your trip
Full cabin controlled heater or air conditioner can be turned on at customer’s request.

restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 8.0 years ?
rows.description: Embark on a scenic nature and cultural adventure in Ketchikan with your own personal naturalist. Your day begins with a visit to Herring Cove, where you'll search for Black Bears in their natural habitat. You'll also have an opportunity to view runs of Pacific Salmon and an array of other wildlife. Enhanced trails through the dense rainforest will lead you on a picturesque walk, enjoying stops at select elevated locations for prime bear viewing. the Carving Center, where a master totem carver crafts totems from local red cedar logs. Finally visit our totem park featuring 17 standing Tsimshian Poles.
highlights: View the abundance of wildlife as you walk through the heart of the dense rainforest.
Observe salmon runs in Eagle Creek and estuary.
Enjoy natural surroundings in Ketchikan in search of Black Bears in their natural habitat in Herring Cove.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: A Misty Fjords Flightseeing
restrictions: Are you between 2.0 and 99.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Experience a bird's-eye view of awe-inspiring Misty Fjords National Monument on a floatplane flightseeing tour that soars above this unbridled terrain. You’ll lift off from a calm body of water and listen to commentary from an expert guide using the plane's headsets. Every guest is guaranteed a window seat, so you won't miss a thing. Look out onto a landscape of cascading waterfalls, alpine lakes, dense forests, snow-capped peaks, and 3,000-foot granite cliffs. For the best possible experience, your flight route is dependent on the day's weather. You may see New Eddystone Rock and Big Goat Lake to the South or Neets Bay and Bell Island Hot Springs to the North. Soar above some of Alaska's most rugged and beautiful territory on this high-flying exploration. You will take off on the water, enjoy a picture perfect 60-minute flight, and return with a water landing.
highlights: Board an Alaskan floatplane for the ride of your life.
Explore the stunning Misty Fjords National Monument.
Get a bird's-eye-view of the awe-inspiring natural landscape.

rows.title: Small Group: Adventure Kart Expedition
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 57.0 inches ?
Are you between 40.0 and 300.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Feel the rush of driving your own custom-designed Polaris RZR 1000 Kart on a private road that will take you deep into Ketchikan’s breathtaking wilderness.
highlights: Drive your own specially-designed adventure kart and get your adrenaline pumping.
Learn about the history, culture, and economy of Ketchikan as you are transported first by motor coach to the rainforest wilderness and the Adventure Kart base camp.

Listen to a safety briefing and gear up in preparation for your exciting, one-and-a-half-hour guided trek. The caravan of karts sets off to explore the remote backcountry along several miles of improved forest trail, with your guide in the lead ATV.
Feel the rush of adrenaline at the start of your journey in a custom designed Polaris RZR 1000 that tests your ability to traverse through the wilderness.

A comfortable pace is maintained as you thread your way around steep curves. The road climbs to reveal unparalleled views of the Tongass National Forest, Behm Canal and Alaska's fabled Inside Passage.
You'll stop along the way to soak up the grandeur and beauty of this lush land. Drivers and passengers will have the opportunity to switch positions for the return trip.
Watch out for wildlife along the way.
Bandanas and Gloves are available for purchase.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Wilderness Exploration and Crab Feast
rows.description: Go ashore at the historic George Inlet Lodge for an all-out Alaskan crab feast. Chart a course for majestic beauty, outdoor adventure, and an incredible meal in the Alaskan wilds. Begin with a coastal drive that passes historic saw mills, waterfalls, and Native Alaskan totem poles. Board a sightseeing vessel and glide in the shadows of snow-capped mountains. View the Mahoney Glacial Cirque and 2,000-foot waterfalls. Keep watch for seals and otters playing in their natural habitat. As the Captain pulls up to the first crab pot, get ready to lend a hand. Join the crew and help haul in the soaked pots to see if there are Dungeness beauties within. After checking the pots, make way to the historic George Inlet Lodge for an authentic Alaskan crab feast. Savor the day’s catch, served with clarified butter and salad with Asian-style dressing. Be sure to leave room for dessert – cheesecake drenched in Alaskan blueberries. During Lunch, try your luck in a customary shell stacking contest. May the best stacker win.
highlights: Cruise by snow capped mountains and keep watch for seals, otters and other wildlife.
Give the crew a hand in pulling the crab pots to see if the day proves to be a lucky one.
Go ashore at the historic George Inlet Lodge for an all-out Alaskan crab feast.

Tour may run in reverse order
Tour may run in reverse. There are alternative meal options available for those that do not like crab.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Exclusive Grizzly Falls Zipline and Marshmallow Roasting
restrictions: Are you between 50.0 and 265.0 lbs ?
Are you greater than or equal to 6.0 years ?
rows.description: Experience the rugged beauty of Alaska firsthand while traversing a thrilling zipline course at Grizzly Falls. Your adventure begins the moment you arrive at the base in Dyea. Here, you'll board a customized Unimog (an all-wheel drive vehicle) that will take you up the mountain road. Once you reach the top, you'll meet your highly trained guides, suit up in state-of-the-art safety gear, and learn how to safely navigate the course before taking off on your heart-pounding experience. Fly across 10 ziplines and four suspension bridges, soaring directly above glacially- fed waterfalls for an up close look at Alaska like you never imagined. After completing the course, you'll enjoy a delicious marshmallow roast around a campfire - the quintessential ending to an amazing day of action and nature in "The Last Frontier ".

Soar over shimmering glacially-fed waterfalls while traversing 10 ziplines and four suspension bridges.
Come down from your adrenaline rush while enjoying a marshmallow roast around an Alaskan campfire.

rows.title: Small Group: Chilkoot Trail Hiking and Rafting
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 7.0 years ?
Are you between 50.0 and 350.0 lbs ?
rows.description: The Canadian Gold Rush Trail, also known as the Chilkoot Trail, was the most direct route prospectors could take to reach the Yukon River during the Klondike Gold Rush. Depart Skagway, once a busy tent city filled with fortune hunters dreaming of untold wealth, and follow in the prospectors' footsteps as you hike the first two gentle miles of their journey. Drink in the beauty and tranquility of the lush rainforest, listen to birdsong and marvel at colorful wildflowers as the trail ascends to Saintly Hill, where gorgeous views await. At the scenic Taiya River, board an 18-foot craft for the peaceful float (no whitewater) to the tidal estuary that offers spectacular views of fjords, snow-capped peaks, hanging glaciers, and fields of flowers. This tour will expand your understanding of this region's dramatic history, even as you marvel at its peaceful stillness today.
highlights: Hike the first portion of the historic Chilkoot Trail, the centerpiece of the Klondike Gold Rush International Historical Park.
Float between majestic snow-capped peaks to the tidal estuary, where the forest gives way to fields of beach greens and wild iris.
Delve into the area's fascinating history as you follow your guide through the lush rainforest, observing wildflowers, colorful birds, and all manner of wildlife.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Tongass Rainforest Trail Expedition
restrictions: Are you between 65.0 and 300.0 lbs ?
Are you between 8.0 and 80.0 years ?
rows.description: Take a walk on the wild side — literally — in Tongass National Forest, and marvel at the ferocious beauty of the Alaskan wilderness. At 500 square miles (the size of West Virginia), ‘the Tongass' is the world's largest temperate rain forest, home to majestic mountains, verdant old-growth forest, fjords, bays, islands and streams. Unspoiled natural surroundings are center stage in this tour. Your small group is met on the dock by a seasoned guide (who will accompany you throughout) for the short drive to the trailhead. Step into the ancient forest, fill your lungs with clean, cool air and allow peace and solitude to envelope you. During the three-mile walk, there will be pauses for photos and wildlife sightings. Listen and watch as your experienced guide shares secrets of the forest and leads you to the best viewing spots. During the salmon-spawning season (May through October) you may see enormous bears scoop fish from rushing streams. The very essence of the Tongass is its towering tree canopy, dominated by evergreens, red and yellow cedar, hemlock, and spruce. Here, nearly every intersection is an animal crossing. Birdsong is the lingua franca. Come to this primal place to regain your sense of awe and childlike wonder.
highlights: Witness nature's creatures — eagles on the wing, spawning salmon, black bear— in their own habitat.
Behold the splendor and magnificence of America's largest national forest, more than 17 million acres of exquisite terrain.
Drink in the forest's sweet clean air and allow its ancient stillness to refresh your body and soul.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Gold Fever and Alaskan Sled Dogs
rows.description: Your adventure begins when you disembark and meet your costumed guide who “lives” in 1898 and is there to accompany you on your Alaskan experience. Your tour includes an amazing encounter with sled dogs and mushers, and a heaping dose of gold fever. Your ticket includes everything you need for a trip to the Klondike. Enter the Gold Rush Training Camp, to learn how to extract paydirt, sluice, and assay your results. Learn the art of gold panning with a humorous and educational show. Practice your technique in heated water troughs. Authentic gold is guaranteed! Weigh your fortune with our assayers. Meet canine athletes that have run the world-famous Iditarod race as well as experienced mushers. Hear about the history of this breed through personal stories and an amazing collection of all the gear necessary on long-distance races like the Iditarod. Observe the musher hooking them up to an authentic sled for a fast practice lap around the track. The next generation of puppies who are there for you to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed! Return to the ship not as a cheechako (newcomer to Alaska) but as a seasoned sourdough (someone who has experienced Alaska and is now a part of its history and culture) with lots of memories to share back home.
highlights: Meet Iditarod dogs and their mushers, and learn about the role sled dogs played in Alaska’s history. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed!
Become a prospector of the Gold Rush era and learn various gold panning techniques and search for authentic Yukon gold at the warm-water troughs, where valuable gold is guaranteed!

rows.description: Your adventure begins with a fully narrated tour of downtown Juneau, Alaska's capital, on the way to Glacier Gardens, 50 acres of rainforest near the Tongass National Park. "The Tongass" comprises the world's last surviving temperate rainforest and the largest national park in the U.S. There, join a knowledgeable forest guide aboard a covered shuttle for the mile-long journey into the heart of the rainforest, which affords breathtaking views of the Mendenhall Valley, carved out by the glacier. Then, expand your understanding with a visit to the Alaska State Museum, which reveals artifacts from the state's rich history, including its Native roots and its time as a Russian territory.
highlights: Explore the Alaska State Museum, which showcases the diverse cultures that have inhabited this rustically beautiful land.
Ascend to a 580-foot elevation and gaze in awe at spectacular views of the Mendenhall Valley, Chilkat Mountains, and Gastineau Channel.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: O Canada! Victoria Highlights
rows.description: Embark on a scenic panoramic drive through Victoria, visiting the city's iconic landmarks, followed by a visit to Tim Hortons where you'll sample Timbits and coffee. Get a taste of Canada on this fascinating scenic drive through picturesque Victoria. Admire local landmarks while learning about all things Canadian. You'll see the Mile Zero Marker, the beginning of the 5,000 mile-long Trans-Canada Highway. Pass Beacon Park with its breathtaking 160-foot tall totem pole. Drive past the lovely Empress Hotel and Parliament Building in Victoria's inner harbor. Then, you'll travel up to Mount Tolmie for spectacular city views. Drive through the University of Victoria, learning about the campus's fascinating American connection. Finally, you'll stop at Tim Hortons for Timbits and coffee—Canadian staples. Enjoy a lively game of Canadian trivia with prizes as you journey through Victoria.

rows.title: Small Group: BC Bites and Sights
rows.description: Your adventure begins with a stroll alongside your entertaining guide, as you explore James Bay, Fisherman's Wharf, and downtown's Inner Harbour by water and land. Enjoy local tastings before boarding your boutique harbor ferry to cruise Victoria's Inner Harbour to downtown. Once downtown, your guide will share fascinating tales about some of the buildings you’ll be seeing, including the British Columbia Legislative Buildings, the Empress Hotel, Bastion Square, and Old Town. This tour is provided by an operator that has been third-party certified to meet the Global Sustainable Tourism Council's Criteria for Tour Operators.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Railroad and Klondike Highway - Multilingual
rows.description: This tour lets you experience two different sides of White Pass while enjoying a narrated commentary in your native language. First, you'll drive along Klondike Highway, one of North America's most scenic roads, through West White Pass in the St. Elias Range. Then, board a vintage railcar and retrace the "Trail of '98" as you journey down the other side of the canyon. Enjoy spectacular views of mountains, glaciers, and waterfalls along the way.
highlights: Drive parallel to the historic Gold Rush Trail of '98 as you travel up Klondike Highway through the West White Pass.
Ride the famous White Pass & Yukon Railroad through the opposite side of the Pass and down the canyon.
Learn about the places you visit in the language of your choosing using a GPS-activated narration.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Secret Garden, City Highlights and High Tea
rows.description: Embrace the natural beauty and royal legacy of Victoria on a journey to one of the top 100 small gardens in the world, complete with traditional high tea, before taking in the city sights and the bustling downtown.
highlights: Arrive to the Abkhazi Garden, created by Georgian Prince and Princess Abkhazi, for exclusive early entrance into this hidden gem teeming with leafy pathways lined with rocky slopes and turtle-filled ponds, in 1946.
Take a serene stroll past Garry oaks, Japanese maples, aromatic roses, 50-year-old rhododendrons, and flowers of every color.
Continue to The Teahouse for Royal Abkhazi high tea and a selection of Silk Road loose leaf teas in a tranquil setting overlooking the gardens.
Delight in a three-tier assortment of scones, quiche, and sandwiches that showcase regional ingredients and produce grown in the surrounding garden.
Afterwards, enjoy a sightseeing drive through the heart of downtown Victoria, where you can explore the city's British Colonial past, souvenir shops, or seafood restaurants on your own.

rows.title: Small Group: Wilderness Dining and Alaska Fishing
rows.description: Fish Alaska's beautiful coastal waters, keeping an eye out for the seals, whales, and spawning salmon as you cast and bears that sometimes amble out of the lush wilderness, and then enjoy the fruits of your labor as a chef uses the fish you caught as entrée in a gourmet meal.
highlights: Gear up in provided waterproof boots, bib overalls, hooded jackets, hat and gloves and sail aboard a 24-foot, open-air skiff.
As you approach the fishing site, stay alert for seals, whales, and spawning salmon that call the area waters home, and for plentiful non-aquatic wildlife like bears, eagles and indigenous birds.
Under your expert guide's supervision, cast your lines into the frigid Alaskan waters and try to land rockfish, cod, or even Pacific salmon that swim in these waters.
With the fishing complete, sail to a remote wilderness campsite, where you can relax by a crackling campfire and enjoy peaceful time beneath towering cedars and spruce.

Indulge in a gourmet meal prepared by a chef who features the fish you caught served atop a saffron-infused, bouillabaisse-inspired stew, as well as sourdough rolls with garlic-aioli spread, signature rhubarb and blueberry crisp, and steaming mugs of coffee or hot chocolate.

rows.title: Small Group: Rainforest Bike and Nature Walk
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 12.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 275.0 lbs ?
Are you between 5.0 and 6.5 feet ?
rows.description: Bike through the raw, untouched beauty of the Tongass National Forest, an Alaskan gem and one of the last intact temperate rainforests in the world.
highlights: A short shuttle ride brings you to the outfitting station to gear up and join your experienced guide on a three-mile biking path through the towering beauty of Tongass National Forest.

Pedal through the immense Alaska beauty, with Sitka spruce and western hemlock high above as you ride through the largest national forest in the U.S., home to rain-soaked mosses, Pacific silver fir, and lichens as well as Sitka black-tailed deer and majestic bald eagles.

Step off your bike at the picturesque Connell Lake Dam that supported the logging industry at Ward Cove Pulp Mill, and continue on a nature walk along a stream popular amongst salmon during spawning season.
Settle in for a fireside snack under the canopy of beautiful, coniferous trees before saddling back up for the return ride along the pipeline trail, taking in the natural sights.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Canadian Ale Trail and City Highlights
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 19.0 years ?
rows.description: Explore the unofficial craft brew capital of Canada on foot as you bend an elbow at three popular breweries with your guides to sample frothy, locally produced beers, each with distinct flavor profiles and histories.
highlights: Depart by bus from the cruise terminal on your narrated tour along the water and past the beautiful Beacon Hill park to the center of downtown Victoria, where you will set out on foot with your local guide and visit three local breweries: Whistle Buoy Brewing Company, Herald Street Brew Works, and Swans Brewery.

At your first stop, Whistle Buoy Brewing Company, in the 19th century mall known as Market Square, sample one of its hoppy IPAs, rich stouts, or crisp lagers, all crafted with care using fresh, local ingredients.

Continue foot through historic Chinatown and Fan Tan Alley, to Herald Street Brew Works, where fine beer standards are complemented by creative recipes, including its popular coffee-infused beer made with locally roasted beans.

Finish by raising a glass at Swans Brewery, where you will enjoy a frothy mug from a brewery known throughout the region for its classic lagers and ales, as well as creative ciders and sours, and satisfy your appetite with an appetizer from its tempting menu.

Afterwards, re-board your bus and continue with a narrated drive through downtown Victoria and the picturesque Inner Harbor, highlighting the Legislative Buildings and the iconic Château-style Fairmont Empress Hotel, before returning to the port.

rows.title: Small Group: Bike and Brew Mendenhall Glacier Tour
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 56.0 inches ?
Are you greater than or equal to 10.0 years ?
rows.description: Set off on a guided bike ride that winds through a glacier carved valley and showcases majestic Mendenhall Glacier from a number of different perspectives. You’ll pedal alongside a local guide on a leisurely exploration that provides an up-close look at Juneau’s wonders. Your ride begins past a view of the Mendenhall Glacier over a stunning beaver dam. Continue along the west side of Mendenhall Glacier and marvel as this natural wonder looms overhead. Stop on the beach to catch your breath before riding along the eastern side of the glacier on a trail through the rain forest. After your ride, relax on a short drive to Merchant’s Wharf and your well-earned beer tasting. Enjoy selection of award-winning beers crafted at different Alaskan microbreweries – a refreshing end to the bike ride of a lifetime.
highlights: Discover Mendenhall Glacier - the largest, most rugged glacier accessible by road.
Savor award-winning beers from a variety of Alaskan microbreweries inside Merchant's Wharf, a landmark in aviation history.
Bike for approximately 8 miles while witnessing Mendenhall Glacier and the surrounding forest.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show and Axe Throwing Challenge
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 10.0 years ?
rows.description: Celebrate southeast Alaska's rich logging history at the rip-roaring good time offered by the Great Alaskan Lumberjack, where premiere woodsmen compete to be the "bull of the woods" and you get to throw an axe without any legal or moral ramifications.
highlights: A quick walk brings you to the Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show, where Alaska's frontier lumberjacks face off against their British Columbian counterparts in a competition featuring chopping, sawing, axe throwing, log rolling and a 50-foot tree climb, all undergirded by the Last Frontier's long and proud longing history.

Know which arm is your axe-throwing arm? You're going to find out as, after a tutorial from an expert, you compete against your fellow tour guests in a friendly competition throwing a five-pound, double-bit axe at a target.
Enjoy the fun and ambiance of this Ketchikan cultural event, and walk back to the port to meet your awaiting ship at your leisure.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Evening Whale Quest with Alaskan Cuisine
rows.description: Embark on an evening cruise where you'll enjoy Alaskan inspired appetizers and with guaranteed whale sightings. Get the most of your day in Juneau, before boarding one of Alaska's best passenger boats. Relax and unwind in the warm, spacious main cabin as you journey into Stephens Passage, a breathtaking marine wilderness surrounded by snow-capped peaks and glaciers. This area is the favored summer feeding ground for humpback whales. Marvel as the whales arc their massive tail flukes into the air. Your captain will navigate the boat close enough to allow exceptional viewing. Learn about these magnificent mammals from your onboard naturalist and discover the other incredible species that call this wilderness home including orcas, sea lions, porpoises, harbor seals, bald eagles and more. The culinary experience highlights locally-sourced cuisine. The menu includes Alaska themed appetizers, seasonal vegetables, meat and cheese tray and various delicious desserts. Complimentary tea, coffee, and cocoa will be available. Locally brewed beer, wine and sodas are available for purchase.
highlights: Listen to an expert onboard naturalist explanation the behavior and habitat of the humpback whale and other wildlife.

Throughout the cruise indulge in locally sourced Alaskan appetizers.

This area is the favored summer feeding ground for humpback whales. Marvel as the whales arc their massive tail flukes into the air. Your captain will navigate the boat close enough to allow exceptional viewing. Learn about these magnificent mammals from your onboard naturalist and discover the other incredible species that call this wilderness home including orcas, sea lions, porpoises, harbor seals, bald eagles and more. The culinary experience highlights locally-sourced cuisine. The menu includes Alaska themed appetizers, seasonal vegetables, meat and cheese tray and various delicious desserts. Complimentary tea, coffee, and cocoa will be available. Locally brewed beer, wine and sodas are available for purchase.

Listen to an expert onboard naturalist explanation the behavior and habitat of the humpback whale and other wildlife.

Try whale sightings from your catamaran's warm, spacious main cabin as you set out to explore Stephen's Passage.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Musher's Camp and Gold Panning
rows.description: Your journey begins with an entertaining historic tour from your knowledgeable guide through downtown Skagway and along nine miles of stunning coastline to our Adventure Camp in Dyea. Kick off your visit with what we like to call a “Musher Talk.” We have been sharing our passion for dog sledding for years, and in that time, we have gathered up the most frequently asked questions about the sport. In the Musher Talk, we will share the fascinating answers and welcome any additional questions you may have. Of course, we make sure you have plenty of time to meet some of the actual athletes who do these amazing things, our sled dogs! After that, have a chance to pan for gold in our riverside sluice! Once your guide shows you the art of gold panning, you can test your luck to see what you can find. Then you will have time to browse the gift shop and relax in our saloon with a deck overlooking the picturesque stream.

Your guide will show you the art of gold panning, test your luck to see what you can find.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Mendenhall Glacier, Whale Watching and Wildlife Quest
rows.description: Experience Alaska’s two most popular attractions – glaciers and wildlife – on one incredible excursion. Begin with a panoramic drive through the Mendenhall Valley to Auke Bay, where you’ll board an Alaskan made expedition vessel for a whale-watching cruise. Relax in the spacious heated cabin as your Captain navigates through the island-studded waters of Stephen’s Passage. You’re most likely to spend time viewing the world famous Humpback Whales and sleek Killer Whales. Also look for porpoises, Bald Eagles, Harbor Seals, Sea Lions, and other wildlife. Afterwards, enjoy a narrated drive to Mendenhall Glacier. Trek along scenic viewing trails for incredible photo opportunities, and learn about the awesome force of glaciers from Forest Service experts at the Mendenhall Visitor Center.
highlights: Explore Alaska’s spectacular wilderness by land and sea on a combination motor coach and catamaran adventure.
Discover the stunning blue-ice Mendenhall Glacier and take in the beauty of Juneau’s unspoiled landscape while traveling through Mendenhall Valley.
Glide along the pristine waters of Stephen’s Passage and enjoy incredible views of snow-capped mountains, towering glaciers, and Alaska’s abundant wildlife.

rows.title: Small Group: Experience Victoria by E-Bicycle
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 16.0 years ?
rows.description: A two-hour sightseeing electric-assisted bicycle ride through Victoria's remarkable streetscapes offers a unique view of the city, as you pedal through Beacon Hill Park along Dallas Road Waterfront Trail through Chinatown and into the Inner Harbour. Meet up with your team of professional rider guides for a quick safety briefing. Then hop on two wheels and ride behind a guide who will lead you along an extensive network of paths and urban bike lanes throughout this "Cycling Capital of Canada". Your sightseeing route lets you explore the south end of Vancouver Island, where you'll admire Victorian architecture and a vibrant city vibe infused with a British colonial past. Breathe in the aroma of colorful flowers while traveling woodland trails through Beacon Hill Park. Feel the salty air rush through your hair along the Dallas Road Waterfront Trail, a recreational area popular with sailboarders and dog walkers. Pass under the ornate Gate of Harmonious Interest, which welcomes you to the oldest Chinatown in Canada. Then direct your e-bike toward the Inner Harbour, a seaside enclave abuzz with boats and float planes. Here you can admire First Nation totem poles and take in the beautiful British Columbia landscape before pedaling back to the pier. This tour is provided by an operator that has been third-party certified to meet the Global Sustainable Tourism Council's Criteria for Tour Operators.
highlights: Embark on a two-wheel, electrically assisted sightseeing adventure through Victoria with an experienced guide who knows the city's best routes.
With more cyclists per capita than any other Canadian city, Victoria offers epic bicycling opportunities.
Pedal past pretty Victorian architecture, Beacon Hill Park, Dallas Road Waterfront Trail, Chinatown's colorful cityscapes, and the Inner Harbour.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Whale Watching and Wildlife Quest
rows.description: Embark on a spectacular sightseeing cruise to discover some of Alaska's most majestic creatures - humpback whales. Your quest begins aboard a waterjet-powered expedition vessel specially designed for wildlife viewing. Relax in the warm, comfortable, spacious main cabin, surrounded by large windows as your experienced captain guides you through the island-studded waters of Stephen's Passage. Against a majestic backdrop of snow-capped peaks and glaciers, the onboard naturalist will explain the behavior and habitat of wildlife you may encounter, including humpback and killer whales, Steller sea lions, Dall's porpoise, harbor seals, bald eagles and more. To fully enjoy the sights and sounds of a close encounter with wildlife, be sure to bring a warm, rainproof jacket to wear on the topside observation (open-air) deck and don't forget your camera! Complimentary snacks and beverages are provided.
highlights: Sail across Stephen's Passage aboard an expedition vessel built in the tour operators very own shipyard.
Gaze upon dozens of Alaska's most elusive wildlife.
Catch spectacular views of snow-capped mountains and glaciers.

rows.title: Small Group: Klondike Summit, Gold Panning and Prospectors Lunch
rows.description: Discover Skagway's rich gold-mining history as you traverse the scenic Klondike Highway aboard a comfortable coach, viewing historic landmarks and vestiges of Gold Rush days along the way. Then meet vivacious pioneer characters, where you'll also try your hand at gold panning. Linger over an open-air rustic feast as you approach a tasty spread of Alderwood-grilled WILD Alaska salmon, Cheechako chicken, Chilkoot baked beans, Tongass wild-rice pilaf, a selection of sides, salads, and beverages. Eat some blueberry cake for dessert as a sweet finish.
highlights: Gaze in awe at majesty of the Alaskan wilderness as you journey across the Klondike Highway.
Follow in the footsteps of old-time gold seekers as you pan for the precious metal.

rows.title: Small Group: Adventure Hike Skagway
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 8.0 years ?
rows.description: Join experienced guides on an exciting hiking adventure through the Tongass Rainforest that immerses you in the incredible beauty of Southeast Alaska. Your adventure will begin with a guided driving town tour of Skagway and continue up the White Pass on the Klondike Highway. Along the way your guide will regale you with history about the Klondike Gold Rush and the natural history of the area. From the trailhead you will spend the next two hours hiking through the beautiful alpine forest surrounded by the towering snow-capped mountains of the White Pass. Your naturalist guides will stop frequently along the way to share their knowledge about the native flora, fauna, and to describe the amazing glacial history of Alaska. Each group is outfitted with binoculars to maximize your opportunities to see up close the spectacular mountain and river scenery. Midway through the hike you will be treated to a delicious snack at the final breathtaking overlook with incredible views of the Skagway valley, White Pass, and the Lynn Canal.

Experience the Tongass National Forest, the nation’s largest rainforest.
Learn about the native flora, fauna, and the glacial history of Alaska

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Whale Watching and Wildlife Tour
rows.description: Experience a remarkably comfortable and captivating whale and wildlife cruise aboard Victoria’s premier whale watching vessels. This high-speed sightseeing vessel affords all sorts of added conveniences. Look out from the open deck or relax in the headed indoor seating area. Enjoy a snack onboard; perhaps even browse a wildlife book from the onboard collection. Keep watch for the telltale spouts of majestic Humpback Whales. Victoria’s waters are also the best place in the world to spot mighty Killer Whales. A team of certified marine biologists travels on every voyage to provide in-depth details about marine mammals and other wildlife found along the way. Cruise through this remarkably pristine territory and have your camera at the ready, as you set sail with Victoria’s largest whale and wildlife spotting fleet.
highlights: Relax aboard the luxurious sightseeing vessel and enjoy a snack during your voyage. Cruise the waters off Victoria in search of Killer Whales, Humpback Whales, porpoises, seals and sea lions, along with other marine wildlife. Learn about whales and the marine ecosystem from a team of marine biologists.

rows.title: Small Group: Ketchikan Pub Crawl
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 21.0 years ?
rows.description: Let's raise a glass to history! This fun, informative and engaging 90-minute walking tour is sure to please anyone who wants to really get to know the local culture of Ketchikan. You'll embark on your pub crawl with a true Alaskan local who will share the colorful history of the bar scene through stories and vintage photographs. You'll hear infamous stories of days gone by, facts of the prohibition era when the red-light district was in full swing and the legacy of many of Ketchikan’s most popular hot spots. You’ll enjoy an adult beverage at each stop, stroll down historic streets and get a real sense of what life is like in this unique rainforest town. Join us and belly up to the bar in Ketchikan!

Hear stories of the Prohibition era and Ketchikan's Red Light District .

rows.description: Feel the rush of riding a bike down the historic Klondike Highway on this thrilling cycling adventure. Your journey begins with a lovely sightseeing drive to the summit of White Pass. Follow the footsteps of fortune-seeking stampeders who travelled this road more than a century ago. Learn about the thousands of gold-seeking stampeders who followed this trail in 1898. Listen to tales of the Klondike’s past and present from your professional guide. Then, feel the rush of riding your bike 15 miles down the historic Klondike Highway. Breathe in the fresh mountain air as you coast down this scenic mountain road. Learn about the ecology of the area from your knowledgeable expert guides. Admire the breathtaking mountains, glaciers, and the historic Yukon Railroad.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Experience Victoria
rows.description: Head out on a one-hour sightseeing coach drive that includes a quick glimpse of Victoria's city highlights and Inner Harbour, plus enjoy optional free time to shop and dine around downtown as you wish. Listen along to an informative guide as you travel in a comfortable bus around the capital of British Columbia. Trace the city's colonial past while viewing its Victorian architecture, Royal BC Museum, and Parliament Buildings. You'll pass through the second-oldest Chinatown in North America and see Mile O of the Trans Canada Highway. Admire the greenery of Beacon Hill Park and the grandiose stature of the luxurious Fairmont Empress Hotel. Along the Inner Harbour waterfront you'll explore the city's First Nations heritage and shipbuilding and commercial fishing industries that thrive at the southern end of Vancouver Island. In the heart of downtown you can either return by bus, traipsing through James Bay, the city's oldest and most charming neighborhoods. Or opt to stay awhile longer, wandering into local shops and eating British Columbian food before making your own way back to the ship. This tour is provided by an operator that has been third-party certified to meet the Global Sustainable Tourism Council's Criteria for Tour Operators.
highlights: Victoria: Enjoy a commentated sightseeing drive past the Parliament Buildings, Royal BC Museum, Fairmont Empress Hotel and Beacon Hill Park.
Inner Harbour: Embrace the city's seafaring spirit while learning about its First Nations roots, shipbuilding industry and commercial fishing operations.
Downtown: Ride through the city's oldest neighborhoods or opt to explore shops and restaurants before returning to the ship independently.

rows.title: Small Group: Talbot Lake Rainforest Hike
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 6.0 years ?
rows.description: Explore the last frontier on an amazing trek through the Tongass National Forest, one of the last intact temperate rainforests in the world, during a hike that invites you to immerse yourself in the immense, unique beauty of Alaska.
highlights: A short drive delivers you into the heart of the Alaskan wilderness and a leisurely hike among the giant coniferous trees of the Tongass National Forest, the largest national forest in the United States.

Walk over the moss-covered grounds of the old-growth forest and listen to the trickle of flowing streams, punctuated by the calls of dozens of birds chirping above as your guide narrates the story of this illustrious natural habitat.
Arrive at the scenic Talbot Lake, where a warm campfire set against the natural backdrop of the surrounding forest awaits.
Recharge with a snack during a quick break, tighten those laces for the return hike to the trail entrance before boarding your shuttle for the return drive to the pier.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Victoria Highlights and History Walking Tour
rows.description: Walk with a knowledgeable local guide to learn about Victoria's scenic, cultural, architectural, and historical highlights on a tour that includes city attractions that can only be reached on foot.
highlights: Meet your guide at the pier and set out to discover Victoria on foot, a tour that’s sure to fascinate and entertain. Along the way, stroll along the scenic oceanfront through historic James Bay, the oldest residential neighborhood in the city. From here, continue to the breathtaking downtown Inner Harbor, a bustling commercial core with regular seaplane departures to the surrounding islands, street performers, local crafts, and food vendors.

The Inner Harbor is also home to the Royal BC Museum, BC Legislative Buildings, and iconic Fairmont Empress Hotel. Walk to Victoria's stunning,19th-century Parliament Buildings, home to British Columbian government since 1897, and get up close in the walk-only areas that take you to the front entrance of these neoclassical gem. Arrive at Thunderbird Park to see its towering totem poles and ornate aboriginal art before continuing to the University of Victoria campus and stopping at the Aboriginal Long House, built in the traditional Coast Salish style. rows.title: Small Group: Ghost and Goodtime Girls Walking Tour
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 16.0 years ?
rows.description: Join a madam from the Red Onion Saloon for a fun and informative walking tour through downtown Skagway! First, you’ll be greeted at the pier by a lavishly costumed and highly entertaining Madam. After a quick shuttle transfer to town, she will guide you through the back alleys and former red-light districts of Skagway. Here, women of the evening brushed shoulders with notorious characters, wide-eyed stampeders, and dangerous odds. Stories of Klondike Kate, Cad Wilson and Diamond Lil depict the adventurous stories of the women who stampeded north. You will pass historic and haunted gold rush buildings as you learn of Skagway’s rich history and the women who helped make it what it is. Your experience culminates with a stop at the world-famous Red Onion Saloon’s restored Brothel Museum. Here, join in a champagne toast and explore the hallways and bedrooms of this celebrated brothel, observing original relics and images of the ladies who worked there. Bring your camera and you may even catch a glimpse of the resident ghost, Lydia! *Your tour concludes at the Red Onion Saloon and guests may choose to stay in town and explore more of Skagway or take the short walk back to the pier.

rows.title: Picture Perfect Victoria
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 16.0 years ?
rows.description: Discover Victoria’s stunning landscapes and vibrant charm on this photographic walking shore excursion that leaves you with both enhanced skills and a gallery of stunning images to treasure.
highlights: Meet your guide at port for a leisurely walk through the historic James Bay neighborhood – one of Victoria’s oldest residential areas – featuring charming, heritage-style homes and streets lined with colorful gardens.
Your guide points out ideal vantage points and offers practical tips for framing shots and using natural light to enhance your images.
Continue your journey along the picturesque Dallas Road, a popular oceanside pathway offering expansive views of the sparkling water and rugged coastline.
Stop at the Mile "0" marker, the symbolic start of the Trans-Canada Highway, for an iconic photo opportunity.

After, arrive at the sprawling Beacon Hill Park, a 200-acre oasis filled with photography inspiration. Your guide leads you to the park’s highlights, such as free-roaming peacocks, manicured gardens, and serene ponds.
Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced photographer, your guide offers hands-on advice for perfecting your technique, from adjusting camera settings to creating engaging compositions.
As your Picture Perfect Victoria excursion concludes, enjoy a relaxed walk back to port with your guide, enjoying any final photo opportunities along the way.

rows.title: Great Fermentations! Wine and Distillery Tour
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 19.0 years ?
rows.description: Undertake a journey of discovery that seamlessly blends the region’s finest wines, spirits, and scenery with its rich history and charm, making it an ideal shore excursion for those seeking to indulge in the best of British Columbia.
highlights: Begin your journey with a scenic drive through Victoria’s idyllic countryside, where rolling vineyards and lush landscapes provide a serene backdrop.
Arrive at a charming local estate winery, renowned for its award-winning vintages and stunning grounds.
After a guided tour, settle into a picturesque tasting room, where you sample a selection of wines, from crisp whites to bold reds, thoughtfully paired with locally sourced cheeses.
Next, travel to a local brewery and distillery, where the focus shifts to craft beer and spirits. Enjoy a tasting of two artisan whiskies, crafted with care and aged to perfection.

After indulging in the countryside’s finest beverages, head back to downtown Victoria for a guided sightseeing tour. Stroll through the charming streets of Old Town, pass by the stately Legislative Buildings, and catch stunning views of the Inner Harbour.
No tour of Victoria would be complete without a visit to the iconic Empress Hotel, a historic landmark known for its grandeur and timeless elegance.
Conclude your Great Fermentations! Wine and Distillery excursion by returning to port, filled with memories of Victoria’s scenic beauty, flavorful discoveries, and rich cultural heritage."

rows.title: Small Group: Kitchen Science Alaska Garden to Table Food Tour
rows.description: Experience a piece of American history – and cuisine - with a visit to the historic Skagway Inn, where you mingle with local Alaskans, immerse yourself in centuries of tradition and discover the culinary secrets of this charming rural community.
highlights: After a brief tour through the city of Skagway, meet with the chef and owner of the historic Skagway Inn and embark on a behind-the-scenes tour of the facilities, as you discover the science that transforms garden-grown ingredients into delicious meals.

Stroll through the inn’s culinary garden and learn about the foods and herbs native to Alaska, along with the techniques used to catch and smoke fresh local fish. Then, head inside the inn, which once served as a brothel during the Klondike Gold Rush.

Here, learn the science behind making a loaf of sourdough bread, as you feed the establishment’s sourdough starter and savor some organic veggies, before transforming simple ingredients from the garden into a sweet concoction – your very own homemade ice cream.

Finish your culinary journey with a hint of luxury, as you enjoy a golden, non-alcoholic drink - a tasty reminder of the town's gold rush past - before returning to your awaiting ship with a few new recipes and skills to bring back home.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Historic Ketchikan Trolley
rows.description: Step aboard a festive trolley and be entertained by your guide’s engaging stories about Ketchikan, renowned as “Alaska’s First City” and the “Salmon Capital of the World.” As you traverse narrow downtown streets, you'll pass by colorful landmarks, bustling boat harbors and the scenic Ketchikan Creek. Your driver guide will share stories that provide a fascinating glimpse into the city's past and present, with special attention to its historical significance and lively atmosphere. Your first stop is the Alaska Rainforest Sanctuary. Discover the beauty of Alaska’s temperate rainforest on this 40-acre private reserve, offering an up-close look at the landscape, wildlife, and culture that make this region so special. Begin with a private stop at Herring Cove, where you might spot harbor seals, bald eagles, and even black bears from a scenic viewing platform. Wrap up your visit with time at the General Store to browse for souvenirs and enjoy a drink at the cozy coffee shop or bar. This relaxed and enriching tour blends natural wonders, wildlife, and Native heritage for a truly memorable experience. Once reboarding the trolley, you have the option of returning to the ship or being dropped off in downtown Ketchikan to explore on your own and return to the ship at your leisure.
highlights: Step aboard a historic trolley and enjoy the colorful sights of Alaska's first city.
View a collection of original Native totem poles highlighting Alaska's indigenous craftsmanship.
Discover how Ketchikan's Native Alaskan heritage and industrial development shaped its history and evolution to the present day.

rows.title: Small Group: Glacier View Sea Kayaking
restrictions: Are you between 40.0 and 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Embark on a scenic sightseeing drive through a historic part of Juneau on your way to North Douglas Island, the launching point of your kayaking excursion. With forested slopes, peaks, and the Mendenhall Glacier as your backdrop, you'll paddle through the waters of Fritz Cove in search of local wildlife. Keep an eye out for eagles, herons, porpoise and even humpback whales. Then enjoy an Alaskan-Style snack before going back to the port.
highlights: Kayak to the Mendenhall Wetlands, a protected habitat that's teeming with more than 40 species of birds and nearly a dozen different mammals.
Explore Fritz Cove and possibly view bald eagles, herons, shorebirds, waterfowl, porpoises and even humpback whales.
Kayak through local waters, getting up-close views of local wildlife and the Mendenhall Glacier eight miles away.
Learn about local flora and fauna, and the history behind landmarks such as Smuggler's Cove. Enjoy a tasty Alaskan snack before boarding the bus back to the port.

rows.title: Small Group: Mendenhall Glacier Canoe Paddle and Trek
restrictions: Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
Are you less than or equal to 80.0 inches ?
Are you greater than or equal to 12.0 years ?
rows.description: Experience the consistent and stunning Alaskan views from both land and water on your journey to the awe-inspiring Mendenhall Glacier. Mendenhall Lake is a treasure trove of natural beauty that rests in front of the glacier. Your guide will outfit you with gear, orient you to your canoe and provide a safety briefing before you paddle toward the Mendenhall Glacier. Glide between the towering cliffs and catch a glimpse of some of Alaska's amazing wildlife. Look for white, small, and slender Arctic Tern seabird nest near the lake. Mountain goats appear high in the mountains along the rocky outcroppings. Watch as billies, nannies and kids make their way to the Rock Peninsula jutting out into the lake. Black bears are also seen occasionally in the brush. As you paddle across Mendenhall Lake, you'll pass floating blue-hued icebergs and the powerful Nugget Falls waterfall cascading down the mountainside. When you reach land, trade your paddling gear for backpacks and trekking poles and absorb the beauty of the beautiful thirteen-mile long river of ice. After landing the canoe at the glacier beach, trade out the paddles for trekking poles and begin the hike over the rocky terrain carved out by the glacier. You will get an up-close and intimate perspective of the glacier, while learning about it’s recession, glacier dynamics, and the breathtaking ice features that unfold from the edge. The Mendenhall Glacier is constantly changing and offers a unique experience each day.
highlights: Canoe paddle: Paddle past icebergs, waterfalls and arctic tern nesting cliffs.
Glacier Views and Hike: Step out of the canoe and hike over the rock terrain carved out by the glacier.
Mendenhall Glacier: Get an up close and intimate perspective of the glacier from the edge, all while learning about the recession of the glacier and the wilderness that surrounds it.
Snacks: Enjoy a tasty light snack of trail mix, nuts, cheese and granola bar.

rows.title: Small Group: Klondike Rock Climbing Adventure
restrictions: Are you between 50.0 and 300.0 lbs ?
Are you greater than or equal to 8.0 years ?
rows.description: Thrill seekers get their fill on a three-hour outdoor Alaskan adventure that includes a scenic Klondike Highway drive and rainforest nature hike, plus adrenaline-rushing rock climbing at White Pass, with all the necessary gear. A 20-minute van ride on the historic Klondike Highway leads you past picturesque lakes and rivers on a trail that prospectors once used during the 1898 Gold Rush. Hop off and follow your guide on an uphill hike through the rainforest across trails teeming with local plants and wildlife. Then meet your professional climbing guides, who will offer a safety briefing and outfit you with all the essential equipment. Climbing shoes, a helmet, and harness will keep you safe. You can choose from over a dozen climbing routes suited for all ability levels, as no experience is necessary. Then get ready for the thrill of a lifetime as you climb a rock face carved out over time by Alaska's mighty glaciers. Take in sweeping views of the river below during the climb and enjoy light snacks and hot cocoa as a tasty way to cap off the action-packed experience.
highlights: Choose from more than a dozen climbing routes, each one rewarding you with an amazing river view.
No experience required.
Enjoy a driving tour of downtown Skagway and a guided hike to the rock climbing site.
This tour offers great climbs and professional instruction for all abilities and skill levels.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Bennett Lake and Yukon Adventure
rows.description: Experience the breathtaking beauty of the North as you travel into the Yukon on this historic railway & motorcoach adventure. Begin your day by traveling via motorcoach to the White Pass Train Depot where you will board the train for the 67-mile journey into the Yukon. Take the unforgettable journey aboard the "Scenic Railway of the World" as you travel from tidewater to the summit of White Pass, a 2,865-foot elevation gain, and onto beautiful Bennett Lake. Relax in a vintage railcar as you pass Bridal Veil Falls, Inspiration Point and Dead Horse Gulch --This fully narrated tour features panoramic views of mountains, gorges, waterfalls, tunnels, trestles, and historic sites. Accessible only by train, Bennett is the site of an abandoned gold rush town located at the end of the Chilkoot Trail on the shores of Bennett Lake. On arrival in Bennett, step off the train and journey back in time as you explore the White Pass historic interpretive center or take a self-guided walking tour up to the church, the views are stunning. Re-board the train and enjoy a hearty box lunch as you travel along the shores of the lake and onto the village of Carcross. Once in Carcross, you will have time to visit the charming community prior to boarding your motorcoach for the trip down the Klondike Highway and back to your ship in Skagway.
highlights: Relax in a vintage railcar along White Pass & Yukon Route Railway, offering stunning window seat views.
Explore the Yukon communities of Bennett and Carcross.
Enjoy lunch onboard the White Pass Railway.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Yukon Adventure and White Pass Scenic Railway
rows.description: Delight in Skagway’s fascinating history and spectacular mountainous scenery as you travel to the White Pass Summit and onward to the border of the Yukon territory. Explore the historic White Pass Trail of '98 from different perspectives. Begin your day ascending into the mountains aboard the White Pass and Yukon Railway. Next, arrive at Fraser, British Columbia, and board a motor coach to continue north through Tutshi Canyon to the border of Yukon territory. Stretch your legs with a photo stop at the "Welcome to Yukon" sign before reboarding your motorcoach.Next arrive at the Yukon Suspension Bridge where you will have time to explore on your own, enjoy a hearty bowl of bison chili and take an optional jaunt across the thrilling suspension bridge for which the venue is named. While at the bridge, experience the sensation of a swaying walkway 57 feet above the rapids of the Tutshi River. Enjoy the scenery from the main platform and then walk through the museum-quality displays that detail the area's unique human and natural history. Finally, your motorcoach will depart down the scenic Klondike Highway. Take in spectacular views of waterfalls and alpine valleys as you make your way back to the ship.
highlights: Ascend into the mountains by train, following the same trail taken by miners more than a century ago.
Enjoy a short photo stop at the historic Yukon Territory sign.
Experience the exhilaration of crossing a 57-foot high suspension bridge.
Enjoy a hearty bowl of bison chili and homemade rolls.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Gold Creek Salmon Bake
rows.description: Treat yourself to a delicious taste of traditional Alaskan cuisine with an authentic salmon bake, as you soak in the natural beauty of Juneau, dine on freshly grilled fish and even try your hand at gold panning.
highlights: A short but scenic shuttle ride brings you to Gold Creek, one of Juneau’s beautiful waterways, where you settle beneath the natural rainforest canopy and savor the enticing aroma of wild salmon grilling over fragrant alder wood.

Groove to the tunes of live music, as you enjoy an Alaskan all-you-can-eat feast that includes all the regional fixings, from barbecue ribs, cornbread, au gratin potatoes, coleslaw and blueberry cake to the star of the show – wild caught and freshly grilled salmon.

After your delicious meal, venture down a nature trail to a nearby, picturesque waterfall, where you can admire the historical ruins of the Wagner Gold Mine. Round out your authentic Alaskan experience by sifting for some gold or roasting a marshmallow over an open camp fire, before returning to your awaiting ship.

rows.title: Small Group: Mendenhall Lake Kayak and Salmon Bake
restrictions: Are you less than or equal to 75.0 inches ?

rows.description: Experience the wonder and majesty of the Mendenhall Glacier on this once-in-a-lifetime kayak journey to Mendenhall Lake, followed by a delicious meal at the Gold Creek Salmon Bake. Enjoy a narrated coach ride to the breathtaking Mendenhall Lake. Here, you’ll meet your guide, receive all equipment and instruction, and begin your kayak adventure. Be one of the select few to paddle across this incredible lake, passing stunning icebergs and viewing Alaskan wildlife in its natural habitat. Venture within a safe distance of the massive, awe-inspiring face of the Mendenhall Glacier. See the breathtaking Nugget Falls. Then, after your thrilling journey, you’ll transfer to Gold Creek Salmon Bake for an all-you-can-eat feast set in the Alaskan rainforest. With a marshmallow roasting pit, majestic waterfall, and mining relics, the Gold Creek Salmon Bake has something for everyone.

rows.description: Experience a true Alaskan adventure while partaking in the official state sport. Take a sled ride through the lush rainforest, play with husky puppies, and learn about this unique element of Alaska’s culture. Finally, cuddle up and help socialize the adorable little huskies.

rows.title: Hop-On Hop-Off City Sightseeing
rows.description: Tap into your inner explorer with this British Columbia shore excursion, as you hop aboard a double-decker bus and explore Victoria's charming landmarks at your leisure.
highlights: Board your double-decker bus and begin your exploration of Victoria - with four stops to choose from and informative audio commentary, the magic of this capital city is your to discover.

Hop off at Cook Street Village, a charming shopping district where you can stroll along tree-lined streets, browse bustling boutiques and take in the relaxed atmosphere at one of the area's many cozy cafe's.

Feeling hungry? Stop at Victoria's Chinatown, the oldest in the counter, and wander the bustling neighborhood, as it comes alive with traditional Chinese architecture and the delicious aroma of fresh Asian cuisine.

Eager for a bit of culture? Pause at the iconic Empress Hotel, a luxurious four-star hotel with chateau-style architecture and a long history of distinguished guests. Just across the street, take in the beauty of the Inner Harbour, a scenic patch of waterfront.
Even more picturesque waterfront views await at the Fisherman's Wharf, which features lively restaurants, brightly-colored floating houses and a unique glimpse into Victoria's maritime heritage.

Once you've satisfied your curiosity, feel free to stay downtown and discover the capital city even further, or bring your Sightseeing City excursion to a close with a bus ride back to you awaiting ship.
