rows.title: Destination Highlight: Whale Watching and Lighthouse Adventure
rows.description: Embark on a whale watching adventure to the historic Sentinel Island Lighthouse in a custom built luxury whale watching vessel. Relax in a heated cabin with outside viewing areas while spotting Humpback whales and other marine life. Upon arrival at the lighthouse, enjoy a short hike on a scenic boardwalk, explore the lighthouse that was built in 1902, and take in the views of the surrounding islands. Next, watch more marine wildlife while enroute to the harbor where your driver-guide will make one final stop at Brotherhood Bridge which offers a stunning view of the Mendenhall Glacier**, before you return to your ship.
highlights: Enjoy a narrated tour to the whale watching harbor.
Watch whales in a comfortable, luxury boats with heated cabins, snacks, and beverages.
Arrive and explore the lighthouse building, property, picturesque boardwalk and views of Lynn Canal and surrounding islands.
Return to the vessel and continue watching whales, sea lions, and bald eagles en-route to the harbor.
Meet up with your driver/guide for a narrated return tour to your ship with a stop at Brotherhood Bridge for a scenic view of the Mendenhall Glacier.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Alaskan Bear Encounter by Land and Sea
rows.description: Experience the massive splendor of Alaskan wilderness as you boat to Neets Bay Observatory to see wild bears in their natural habitat, feasting on salmon in a pristine, seldom-visited environment.
highlights: Enjoy Alaska's sublime natural beauty on a narrated drive to Clover Pass Resort to join an experienced captain and crew on a boat setting sail along the magnificent coast, passing Tongass National Forest on your way to Neets Bay Observatory, a pristine wilderness area that is limited to a small number of visitors each year, including you.

Enjoy the serenity of the verdant forest as you walk along a well-maintained forest trail to a viewing platform and listen as your guide discusses the resident bear population, the surrounding forest, and the importance of hatcheries to enhance salmon production in southeast Alaska.
Have your camera ready for the once-in-a-lifetime experience of co-existing with these incredible creatures as the bears teach their young to fish or relax after a big meal of fresh salmon.
After, board the boat for the return trip and another glimpse of Last Frontier scenery before returning to the port to meet your awaiting ship.

rows.title: Small Group: Rainforest Canopy and Zipline Adventure
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 57.0 inches ?
Are you between 70.0 and 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Glide through the top of a rainforest canopy along a series of cables suspended between tall hemlock, spruce, and cedars and gain a unique perspective on the rainforest surrounds. This adventure excursion combines a flight over 2400 feet of high tension cable, guaranteed to thrill all.
highlights: Depart the pier for the brief transfer to Herring Cove, located within the borders of the magnificent Tongass National Forest. This major rainforest reserve embraces one of Alaska's richest salmon spawning streams and a pristine inter-tidal estuary. Abundant wildlife habitat here includes a high concentration of bald eagles, black bear, and a variety of other species.
At Herring Cove, an all-terrain custom 4x4 vehicle transports you up a steep rain forest hillside to the canopy chalet.

Professionally trained guides outfit you with state-of-the-art safety equipment and provide a detailed orientation before you mount the first platform. Your rainforest canopy challenge features a flight over nine ziplines, an aerial bridge suspended between tall spruce, hemlock and cedar trees, and ground-based nature trails.
Your guide will progressively ease you into the forest canopy along cables ranging from a short 100 feet up to 600 feet with platform as high as 100 feet above the ground.
Eagles and bears are often seen from the platforms.
Cross a hanging bridge that will lead you to our longest zip that breaks out into the canopy offering breathtaking views of the forest and salmon spawning creek below.
Throughout this adventure, you will be surrounded by the sights and sounds of the rain forest wildlife preserve.

The final zip-line emerges from the rain forest and transports you over lush grassy wetlands to a viewing tower overlooking the Whitman Lake Fish Hatchery, 55 feet above the ground where you will gently descend to the forest floor.

Time permitting, grab a freshly brewed coffee or a cold Alaskan beer and explore our Tsimshian totem park, see a master carver working, check out our historic sawmill and watch our local blacksmiths at work.
Stop by the General Store to see photos taken during your zip-line experience and to shop for mementos of your Alaska adventure.
Your excursion concludes with a scenic drive back to the ship.

rows.title: The Local Table - Hands on Salmon Cooking Class
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 8.0 years ?
rows.description: Immerse yourself in the rich flavors and traditions of Alaska with a hands-on wild salmon cooking shore excursion along the picturesque Tongass Narrows.
highlights: Begin your culinary journey with a short walk to The Local Table – a cozy venue overlooking the serene waters of the Tongass Narrows.
Greeted by your instructor, a third-generation fisherman with deep ties to Alaska’s fishing heritage, feel right at home as the warm aromas of fresh seafood and spices fill the air.
Under the expert guidance of your instructor, dive into the preparation of three simple yet extraordinary salmon dishes: classic grilled salmon, smoked salmon dip, and salmon cakes with two sauces.
The class features hands-on cooking stations, where you chop, marinate, and assemble alongside fellow participants.
Once the cooking is complete, gather around the communal dining table to enjoy the fruits of your labor.

The Tongass Narrows provides a stunning natural backdrop as you indulge in your homemade meal. Watch as float planes glide over the water, fishing boats return to the harbor, and wildlife, like seals or eagles, make the occasional appearance.
After your meal, conclude your Hands on Salmon Cooking Class with a satisfied palate, a wealth of new culinary skills, and a recipe booklet to recreate the dishes at home.

rows.title: Explore Wild Adventure Yukon, Sled Dogs and Scenic Highway
rows.description: Embark on a thrilling adventure that combines history, stunning landscapes, and the spirit of the gold rush as you journey up the legendary White Pass Summit. Your experience begins with a comfortable, narrated motor coach ride through the charming streets of downtown Skagway, where you’ll pass historic wooden boardwalks and the preserved façades of this once-bustling frontier town. As you leave the town behind, you're driver guide will share stories of Skagway's colorful history while you retrace the steps of the brave prospectors who risked everything in search of fortune during the Klondike Gold Rush. As you climb the summit, 3,292 feet above sea level, you’ll be awe-struck by breathtaking views of the rugged landscape, sweeping vistas of snow-capped peaks and glacial-fed rivers.
highlights: Enjoy a guided drive through downtown Skagway and up the White Pass Summit, hearing stories of Skagway settlers during the Gold Rush.
Indulge in an all-you-can-eat Alaskan BBQ chicken buffet with all the fixings.
Enjoy a variety of activities fun for the whole family at Wild Adventure Yukon, including a sled dog experience.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Potlatch Park and Lumberjack Show
rows.description: Experience the rich native history of Ketchikan and enjoy a taste of the enduring spirit that pioneered this Alaska’s first wilderness city. Your fun-filled day begins in hardy fashion at the Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show. Enjoy a taste of pioneer days as you watch logging history come to life in the form of competitions, exhibitions and awesome displays of rugged skill. From here, you’ll head to the spectacular Potlach Totem Park, where Ketchikan’s native culture is celebrated in grand fashion. Enjoy a guided tour through this fascinating collection of totem poles and witness the tradition tools used to create these timeless reflections of Native Alaska. Afterwards, relax on a narrated drive that winds along the Ketchikan waterfront, and showcases the salmon canneries along the North Tongass Highway. Discover Ketchikan on a tour that reveals the heart and soul of Alaska’s first city. THIS TOUR IS OPERATED BY A TOUR OPERATOR THAT HAS BEEN THIRD-PARTY VERIFIED TO AN INTERNATIONALLY RECOGNIZED SUSTAINABILITY STANDARD.

rows.title: Small Group: Hunting for Halibut
restrictions: Are you between 5.0 and 90.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 300.0 lbs ?
rows.description: No matter your skill level, you will fish like a local, working the sandy flats or sea floor pinnacles with your rod in hand, drifting with the current and jigging in waters as deep as 300 feet. Species availability changes throughout the season, and daily factors such as tides and wind affect your fishing opportunities. This multi-species Alaskan fishing expedition allows you to head out with the confidence that you will catch fish. Down there in the twilight of the deep, rockfish, cod, flounder, sharks, skates, and mighty halibut find their way to your bait. No matter what the conditions or the catch, this trip offers the best chance of exposing you to the strange and alien species found on the Southeast Alaskan deep-sea floor. Historically 50% of our guests catch Pacific Halibut. 2026 updated regulations: Daily bag limit of one halibut per guest. Max lower size limit of 34”. As well, there is an upper limit fixed at 80” or greater for all 2026 dates.
highlights: Deliver your halibut and a purchase of local Alaskan seafood shipment to your home for an additional charge.
Pursue the elusive halibut with an experienced guide while learning the secrets of working the tides.
Board a Raider Pro Fisherman and experience an anglers dream come true - fishing for Pacific halibut in Alaska.

As well, there is an upper limit fixed at 80” or greater for all 2026 dates.

Deliver your halibut and a purchase of local Alaskan seafood shipment to your home for an additional charge.

Pursue the elusive halibut with an experienced guide while learning the secrets of working the tides.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Lighthouse, Totems and Eagles
rows.description: Your Alaskan captain and their crew will share Southeast Alaska's coastal history, Native culture, and natural beauty as you journey into Ketchikan's wild seascape on a wildlife-viewing cruise, with guaranteed sightings of American bald eagles. Discover the beauty of the Tongass National Forest, the world's largest temperate rainforest, and learn about Alaska's interconnected ecosystems and the animals that call this region home. Enjoy panoramic views of Totem Bight State Park from the water, including an Alaska Native clan house and a collection of 14 towering Tlingit and Haida hand-carved cedar totem poles. Breathe in the fresh sea air as you cruise to Guard Island, where you'll have the opportunity to photograph the first lighthouse to be built in Alaska, completed in 1904 and still in operation today. Sip a hot chocolate and keep on the lookout for harbor seals, moon jellyfish, pigeon guillemots, deer, blue heron, whales, and other wildlife.

Traverse the untouched glacial icefield on foot.
Relish in the mystery of Mendenhall Valley with a bird's-eye-view of mountains, forests and wilderness.

rows.title: Small Group: Last Frontier Food Tour
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 12.0 years ?
rows.description: Eat your way through Juneau and you'll come to know Alaska's capital city in a new and meaningful way. Your culinary host, who is a local resident, will share the best of Alaskan food, as well as local legend and lore, on this leisurely walking tour through the culinary heart of the city. Stops include award-winning restaurants and you will often meet the chefs and restaurant owners who are making Juneau a sought-after dining destination. Your menu includes king crab bisque, fresh salmon, Alaskan game, the famous hog wing, and tea from indigenous plants, earning the "Made in Alaska" seal of approval. Your experience wraps up at an historic bar, where you will sample three beers from a local brewery. This experience focuses on connecting you with the city and with Alaska, and you're sure to come away with a deeper understanding and tasty memories of your time in Juneau.

rows.title: Small Group: Exclusive Alaskan Whale Discovery
rows.description: It's a seafaring safari! Watch humpback whales, Steller sea lions, Dall's porpoise, orcas and harbor seals up close on this small group experience. A safari vessel, low to the water for a favorable vantage point, hosts fully guided, small-group whale-watching adventures on a voyage through Stephens Passage, a waterway known for plentiful whale activity. A professional naturalist guide leads the group through hands-on activities that support research into the entire ecosystem, such as monitoring for invasive-species, humpback-whale identification and marine mammal observations. Pull a crab pot with your guide to explore and learn more about the critters roaming around Juneau’s Ocean floor as this data is used to give back to local research and conservation efforts. The time in Stephens Passage provides a transformative opportunity not only to see whales and enjoy their antics, but to deepen one's understanding of delicate marine environments and the creatures that exist in them.

An exclusive experience on a smaller-sized whale-watching vessel allows you to get up close with Alaska's wild beauty.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: A White Pass Scenic Railway
rows.description: Sit back and take in the vast beauty of Alaska aboard what is arguably the most scenic railway in the world. The narrow-gauge White Pass & Yukon Railway is a monumental feat of engineering built in 1898 and listed among the International Historic Civil Engineering Landmarks. You’ll board a vintage railcar that’s been retrofitted for sightseeing comfort and begin a 20-mile journey from the tidewater up an incredible 2,865 feet to the summit of White Pass. Outside your window, witness the original “Trail of 98”, where brave stampeders trekked during the Klondike Gold Rush. Relish in the natural splendor of the vast wilderness as you journey through a landscape of sky-high trestles, plummeting gorges, and expansive valleys. Marvel at the beauty of Bridal Veil Falls and a breathtaking panorama, aptly named, Inspiration Point. Step back in time and rumble into unspoiled Alaska aboard the White Pass & Yukon Railroad.
highlights: Climb aboard the White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad, built in 1898.
Sit back in a restored vintage railcar as you wind through the majestic Alaskan wilderness.
Follow the same route take by stampeders during the legendary Klondike Gold Rush.

rows.title: Small Group: Taku Glacier Helicopter and Airboat Adventure
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 7.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Venture into the heart of Alaska’s wilderness by air, water, and ice! This is a rare opportunity to experience the Taku Glacier from all possible perspectives. Begin your adventure with an aerial exploration of the breathtaking Juneau Icefield and surrounding Tongass National Forest before touching down at the isolated Taku Point. Continue with an hour-long airboat expedition of the area and experience close-up views of the Taku Glacier as you cruise its 5-mile terminus. Next, fly up to the surface of the icefield for your brief glacier landing With viewing opportunities of the glacier’s dramatic face and deep blue crevasses, this is sure to be a truly unforgettable Alaskan experience.
highlights: Learn about the Alaska wilderness from a knowledgeable guide as you fly above the Tongass National Forest.
Land on the Taku Glacier, the thickest and deepest of the word's glaciers, accessible only by air and water.
Face the glacier head on and close up from an airboat in the Taku River.

rows.title: Small Group: Last Frontier Zip Lines and Adventure Park
restrictions: Are you between 50.0 and 300.0 lbs ?
Are you greater than or equal to 6.0 years ?
rows.description: Fly high over the Alaskan rainforest canopy on this adrenaline-packed zip line adventure. Your journey begins with a scenic drive through Skagway. Travel up into the mountains on the historic Klondike Highway. Then, at the nature park, enjoy a short rainforest hike, arriving at the thrilling attractions. Meet your expert guides, gear up, and get ready to fly. Soar high over the rainforest canopy on six exhilarating zip lines. Scramble over a series of Burma loops, cross harrowing suspension bridges, and challenge yourself to walk over the raised balance beams. Test yourself with the ultimate challenge at the park and “walk the plank.” Fly over a zip line that soars where only the eagles dare. After your zip line adventure, warm yourself with a cup of hot cocoa, tea, coffee, and dispensable water will be available as well.

o Drive through downtown Skagway and along the Klondike Highway to the starting point of your nature hike.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Haines Wilderness Odyssey by Jet Boat
rows.description: After a scenic ferry ride, you'll arrive in the quaint little town of Haines to begin your Alaskan wilderness exploration. Board a flat-bottom riverboat and glide across the Chilkat River, keeping your eyes peeled for moose, bears, and other local wildlife. If you're lucky, you may even see a massive eagle's nest in the Cottonwood trees. Then, enjoy a hearty packed lunch in a picnic-style setting before heading back to Skagway.
highlights: Take a 45-minute ferry ride to the town of Haines and explore the Bald Eagle Preserve.
Travel many channels of theChilkat River on a specially designed riverboat and explore the wilderness in comfort.
Watch the rivers edge for wildlife, as the presence of the boats at times draws them out with curiosity.

rows.description: On this tour, you’ll take Skagway’s famous White Pass and Yukon Route Railroad along the eastern side of the White Pass to the 2,865-foot summit. Journey down the west side of White Pass canyon in the comfort of a motorcoach passing several landmarks including Dead Horse Gulch, Pitchfork Falls, and the beautiful Tormented Valley. Along the way, stop at Alaska 360 Dredge Town to learn more about modern dog sled racing before meeting a team of sled dogs.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: VIP Endicott Arm Fjord and Glacier Explorer
rows.description: Experience Alaska's majestic Endicott Arm Fjord, including up-close views of awe-inspiring Dawes Glacier from the comfort and heated cabins of an ultra-modern Alaskan built vessel. On this premier experience guests will be able to enjoy the grandeur of Endicott Arm while enjoying breakfast, lunch and complimentary beverages.
highlights: Board the Alaskan built vessel and find a great viewing spot from the heated cabins with large windows and set out to Endicott Arm Fjord, the southern edge of Fords Terror Wilderness area and a popular area for seals.
Maneuver close to the ever-changing face of Dawes Glacier, more than 600 feet tall and a half-mile wide, and known for its spectacular calving displays which produce huge, chunky icebergs.
Cruise beneath ice cliffs that rise 4,000 feet above the sea, so close you can feel the mist of a waterfall on your face.
Keep your phones and cameras handy for photos of the pigeon guillemots, bald eagles, harbor seals, mountain goats and bears that frequent the area.
Enjoy a continental breakfast, unlimited hot and cold drinks, a picnic-style lunch and a complimentary “Glacier Blue” margarita during our journey.

Before concluding your Station One Distillery and Bottle Infusion excursion, your recipe is saved on file, ensuring you can reorder your signature creation for years to come.
Take a moment to celebrate your accomplishment with fellow participants and raise a glass to the unforgettable journey into distilling.

rows.title: Small Group: Glacier Dogsledding Demonstration by Helicopter
rows.description: Fly deep into Alaska's glacier wilderness to meet the remarkable sled dogs and mushers of the Iditarod. Land on remote Denver Glacier, hear trail stories, watch an exciting dogsledding demonstration, and cuddle future champions on this unforgettable small‑group adventure.
highlights: After a short drive from port, begin your journey at the helicopter base in Skagway, where you check in and gear up.
Board your helicopter and take to the skies for an awe-inspiring flight above the Taiya Inlet, weaving through towering mountain peaks, cascading waterfalls, and lush alpine forests.
After landing gently on Denver Glacier, step out and be greeted by an experienced musher and their team of eager sled dogs.
The musher shares fascinating stories about their life on the trail, the grueling Iditarod Race, and the incredible bond between musher and dog.
Watch as the musher demonstrates how these elite canine athletes work together to pull a sled across the glacier.
After the demonstration, meet the future champions—adorable sled dog puppies! Take time to cuddle with these playful pups and snap photos with them against the pristine glacier landscape.

Conclude your Glacier Sled Dog Experience excursion with another helicopter ride back to Skagway. Upon landing, de-gear and take a moment to savor the memories of this extraordinary day, before heading back to your ship.

Small Group: Glacier Dogsledding Demonstration by Helicopter

Fly deep into Alaska's glacier wilderness to meet the remarkable sled dogs and mushers of the Iditarod. Land on remote Denver Glacier, hear trail stories, watch an exciting dogsledding demonstration, and cuddle future champions on this unforgettable small‑group adventure.

After a short drive from port, begin your journey at the helicopter base in Skagway, where you check in and gear up.

Board your helicopter and take to the skies for an awe-inspiring flight above the Taiya Inlet, weaving through towering mountain peaks, cascading waterfalls, and lush alpine forests.

After landing gently on Denver Glacier, step out and be greeted by an experienced musher and their team of eager sled dogs.

The musher shares fascinating stories about their life on the trail, the grueling Iditarod Race, and the incredible bond between musher and dog.

Watch as the musher demonstrates how these elite canine athletes work together to pull a sled across the glacier.

After the demonstration, meet the future champions—adorable sled dog puppies! Take time to cuddle with these playful pups and snap photos with them against the pristine glacier landscape.

Conclude your Glacier Sled Dog Experience excursion with another helicopter ride back to Skagway. Upon landing, de-gear and take a moment to savor the memories of this extraordinary day, before heading back to your ship.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Rainforest Wildlife Sanctuary, Eagles and Totems
rows.description: Delve into the heart of Ketchikan's rainforest on a scenic eco-tour at an exclusive reserve, led by a naturalist. Begin your day at Herring Cover, following your naturalist along a nature trail through dense forest and across tall stands of hemlock, spruce, and Alaska cedar. Enjoy stops at picture-perfect viewing areas along the way. At Eagle Creek, a protected estuary, keep an eye out for local wildlife, including bears and seals during salmon spawning season. Also watch for mink, Marten, and wolves.
highlights: Embark on a scenic eco-tour, complete with a nature hike a native totem carver, totem park and historic sawmill.
Follow Eagle Creek at an open estuary along an elevated wooden boardwalk overlooking grassy wetlands.
Visit Herring Cove, home to eagles, Black Bears, seals, and a variety of other species.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Ketchikan Duck Tour
rows.description: Discover the history and beauty of Ketchikan, the “Salmon Capital of the World” by land and sea, aboard our Duck amphibious vehicle. Your journey begins with a scenic drive through Ketchikan’s rustic streets. View the salmon ladder on Ketchikan Creek, seeing the spawning grounds and live salmon in season. Learn about the importance of the salmon industry to the region. Then, pass breathtaking Whale Park with its lovely flowers and unique totem poles. See Creek Street, where you’ll hear tales of Ketchikan’s colorful past and salty beginnings. Then, marvel as your bus becomes a boat and your Duck bus literally drives into the scenic harbor of the Tongass Narrows. Pass the local fishing fleet. Motor down the channel for up-close views of canneries, seaplane docks, and the Ketchikan waterfront community. Take in the breathtaking scenery of southeast Alaska’s temperate rainforest, looking out for bald eagles and other majestic wildlife.
highlights: Explore Ketchikan's most historic regions and rustic charms.
Travel past the Salmon ladder on Ketchikan Creek and catch fish leaping upstream.
Discover beautiful Whale Park with its striking totem poles and stunning flowers.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Bering Sea Crab Fishermen's Tour
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 5.0 years ?
rows.description: Set out to sea aboard the Aleutian Ballad, a Bering Sea crab boat, for an adventure that’s so carefree even landlubbers can do it. This actual boat was featured on season two of the Discovery Channel series, Deadliest Catch, and it’s seen some serious rolling seas and massive rogue waves. Fortunately, that won’t happen today. Instead, you’ll cruise calm waters as the fishing crew demonstrates the art of crabbing and long line fishing. Watch as massive crab pots are hauled on deck. Plus, check out the unique octopus pots, and keep your eyes peeled as long lines are pulled from the deep to see what might be on the other end. During the voyage, you’re also likely to spot wildlife, including majestic Bald Eagles, so keep those cameras ready. This award-winning excursion gives you a fun, safe and easy taste of the hard-earned life on the Bering Sea.
highlights: Enjoy stadium-style seating on your quest across the Inside Passage.
Discover amazing wildlife, including eagles, crabs, and octopus.
Learn about commercial fishing and the fishermen's unique adventures.

rows.title: Destination Highlights: Misty Fjords and Wilderness Explorer
rows.description: Experience the wilds of Misty Fjord(s) on a cruise that’s both awe inspiring and eye-opening. Go exploring aboard one of the fastest and most luxurious sightseeing vessels in Alaska. Cruise past the float planes and fishing boats of Ketchikan Harbor and throttle up for a fun-filled run along the rugged coastline bordering Revillagigedo Channel. Enter Behm Canal and enjoy complimentary snacks as you glide past the pristine beauty of Misty Fjords, a land the great naturalist and environmental philosopher, John Muir, once coined, “the Yosemite of the North.” Along the way, stop to view a Bald Eagle nest and a Tlingit pictograph. Witness Eddystone Rock, a volcanic wonder rising from the sea and delight in a leisurely cruise through Rudyerd Bay, an ice carved masterpiece. Journey beneath 3000-foot-high cliffs, sculpted by glacier movement, as your expert guide talks about the history, geology and wildlife of this breathtaking landscape.
highlights: Explore the mysterious Rudyerd Bay, surrounded by 3,000-ft high granite cliffs.
Glide beneath miles of icy mountain tops and spectacular evergreen forest canopies.
View wildlife and learn about the natural history of the area.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Knudson Cove Salmon Fishing
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 5.0 years ?
rows.description: Don't miss this highlight of your Alaskan Cruise - fishing for salmon. Experience a narrated tour of the city and outlying areas, learning about the history and lifestyle of Ketchikan from a seasoned and knowledgeable charter Captain. What better way to spend your time in the 'Salmon Capital of the World'? The Knudson Cove Marina welcomes the opportunity to show you, your family and friends the charm and majestic beauty of Southeast Alaska. Located just 30 minutes from downtown Ketchikan, the marina boasts a locale on prime fishing grounds. You will have about 3 hours of fishing, and due to its convenient location, you'll only be a few minutes from the dock before your lines hit the water. With only 4 to 6 passengers assigned to each fishing boat, this fleet offers you the chance for individualized attention. Whether you're an experienced angler or a novice, your Coast Guard licensed Captain will treat you to a memorable excursion as he shows you the 'ins' and 'outs' of fishing in Alaska. In between reeling in your catch, you can marvel at the wildlife – seals, sea lions, otter, porpoise, and even whales might treat you to a show, while bald eagles will offer you a superb photo opportunity as they swoop down to grasp their own "catch of the day." The type of fishing that you'll be doing is trolling for salmon with downriggers. Downriggers are used to hold the fishing lines deep in the water. Most of the time 4 to 5 rods will be used and boats hold 4-6 persons. The fishing lines will be clipped or connected to the downrigger system and then the lines are dropped to a specified depth using a large weight that is attached to the downrigger. The poles will be set or held in rod holders while the boat trolls or moves along. When a fish strikes, the line will come free of the downrigger the guest will then take the rod out of the rod holder, set the hook and play the fish. After a fish is played and landed guests will be asked to rotate over one position, sometimes one pole and type of lure catches the majority of the fish and by rotating everyone then has a fair chance of catching. To ship the daily catch home - Custom Fish Processing is available for an additional charge. Major credit card required.
highlights: Embark on a guided fishing charter into the fertile waters of Knudson Cove.
Set out your lines and get ready to real in your fresh salmon catch.
Keep your eyes peeled for seals, whales, bald eagles and other Alaskan wildlife.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Ancient Totem Poles and Rainforest Canoe Ride
rows.description: Discover the history and rugged beauty of "The Last Frontier " as you embark on an Alaskan adventure in Ketchikan that showcases authentic totem poles, Native culture, and culminates with an exciting canoe ride across a pristine mountain lake. Your day begins at Totem Bright State Park, located on the former site of a traditional Native campground. Trace a path back to the early days of the region while marveling at an array of remarkable totem poles and artifacts. After, you'll head to Lake Harriet Hunt, where you'll learn about the ancient tools that were used to carve incredible canoes, then board a canoe of your own and paddle across the lake to a local camp for an Alaskan-style lunch and scenic nature walk.
highlights: Immerse yourself in the art, culture, and traditions of Southeast Alaskan Native culture as you explore Totem Bight State Park.
Travel like the native Alaskans once did, as you glide across Lake Harriet Hunt in an easy-to-paddle, 37-foot Native-style canoe.
Sample Alaskan-style fare, including clam chowder, smoked salmon, and wild berry jam.

restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 50.0 lbs ?
rows.description: River rafting, icebergs and a snack! Your experienced guide will steer you across the serene waters of Mendenhall Lake, past floating icebergs to the Mendenhall River. Here you'll float a 5-mile section of a glacial stream which includes short stretches of mild to moderate rapids. Enjoy spectacular views of the Mendenhall Glacier and soak in the amazing scenery. Learn about the history of the river and lookout for wildlife.

rows.title: Small Group: Historic Gold Panning and Gold Creek Salmon Bake
rows.description: Enjoy a tour of Juneau including the state capitol, before arriving at Salmon Creek and the historic Wagner Mine to pan for gold. Learn to pan in the creek, or in the waist high demonstration trough. Then, enjoy an all you can eat adventure at Gold Creek Salmon Bake. A Juneau culinary tradition for more than 30 years. After your meal stroll under the rainforest canopy to the lovely Salmon Creek waterfall, roast marshmallows over an open campfire, or enjoy a piece of fresh baked blueberry cake.

rows.title: Small Group: Helicopter Glacier Trek
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 12.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Embark on a guided exploration of Juneau’s icefields that brings you both a big-picture and intimate perspective. Begin with a helicopter flightseeing tour that soars high above the frozen landscape. Marvel at a bird’s-eye view of the Alaskan wilds and the grandeur of Juneau’s glacial wonders. Touch down at the launch site for a 2-hour hike through this foreboding landscape. All the gear you need to keep safe and warm will be provided, and your mountaineering guide will get you prepared with expert instruction before the group sets out. You’ll trek to remote areas of the glacial landscape that few people ever witness. Along the way, your guide will provide fascinating commentary about glacial formation and movement. No climbing experience is required to participate in this exploration. Simply bring your love of the great outdoors and your guides will provide the basic skills needed for an awe-inspiring adventure.
highlights: Enjoy a panoramic perspective of Juneau’s “icescapes” on a 30-minute helicopter flightseeing tour.
Land and gear up for a 2-hour guided hike across the glacial terrain.
Trek to places few people ever witness while your expert guide provides insight into the glacial environment.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Gold Fever, Alaskan Sled Dogs and Exclusive Scenic Railway
rows.description: Enter the Gold Rush Training Camp, to learn how to extract paydirt, sluice, and assay your results. Learn the art of gold panning with a humorous and educational show. Practice your technique in heated water troughs. Authentic gold is guaranteed! Weigh your fortune with our assayers. Meet canine athletes that have run the world-famous Iditarod race as well as experienced mushers. Hear about the history of this breed through personal stories and an amazing collection of the gear necessary on long-distance races like the Iditarod. Observe the musher hooking them up to an authentic sled for a fast practice lap around the track. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed! The adventure continues with your journey aboard the world-famous narrow gauge White Pass and Yukon Route Railroad to the 2,865-foot summit. Your guide will help with narration, some fun games, and a toast and snack at the summit all on a private train car just for your group.
highlights: Travel in style aboard the White Pass Scenic Railway and enjoy eye-popping views en route to the summit.
Meet Iditarod dogs and their mushers, and learn about the role sled dogs played in Alaska’s history. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed!
Become a prospector of the Gold Rush era and learn various gold panning techniques and search for authentic Yukon gold at the warm-water troughs, where valuable gold is guaranteed!

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Mendenhall Glacier and Salmon Bake
rows.description: Embark on an excursion that takes you to the most popular landmark in Juneau and wraps up with an Alaskan-style salmon feast. Enjoy a scenic drive to Mendenhall Glacier, the world’s largest glacier accessible by roadway. Gaze out from the vantage point at the Mendenhall Glacier Visitor Center and marvel at one of North America’s most awe-inspiring wonders. Learn about glacial movement and the immense force of the Mendenhall from the knowledgeable experts at the Visitor Center. From here, you’ll travel to a lovely spot in the Alaskan rainforest for an al fresco salmon bake. Relax to musical entertainment and warm up in the covered and heated pavilion. Or, go for a trek to find a nearby creek, waterfall, and the remnants of an abandoned mine. Then, it’s time to savor a feast of alder wood-grilled salmon, barbecue ribs, and an assortment of sides sure to satisfy the heartiest wilderness appetite.
highlights: Enjoy a scenic drive to Mendenhall Glacier, the crown jewel of the Juneau ice field.
Take in spectacular views and capture stunning photos of this natural wonder and national treasure.
Savor an outdoor Alaskan salmon bake, featuring alder wood-grilled salmon and all the trimmings.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Alaska State Museum and Salmon Bake
rows.description: It's off to the Alaska State Museum, which is newly renovated, to see the fascinating exhibits and enjoy a journey through the state's history. Then, head to the Salmon Bake and indulge in a feast under an outdoor canopy in the rainforest. Savor wild grilled Alaskan salmon, grilled chicken, Caesar salad with salmon, coleslaw, baked beans, rice pilaf, cheesy potatoes, and cornbread, topped off with blueberry cake and accompanied by lemonade, tea, and water. The tour ends at the Salmon Bake and guests can stay as long as they wish (and eat as much as they like). When you're ready to return to the ship, hop on a shuttle, which leaves every 15 minutes.
highlights: Explore the Alaska State Museum, which showcases the diverse cultures that have inhabited this rustically beautiful land.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Whale Watching Adventure and Salmon Bake
rows.description: Explore "The Last Frontier" aboard a jet-powered catamaran specially designed for Alaskan waters. Keep an eye out for humpback whales during the 2.5-hour tour as they are actively feeding in Alaskan waters at tour time, you are guaranteed to see them breach, play, bubble-net feed or otherwise make an appearance. Then, savor the Gold Creek Salmon Bake, a delicious 40-year tradition. Relax in these magnificent natural surroundings while you feast on wild-caught grilled Alaskan salmon, barbecue ribs, reindeer sausage, numerous side dishes, salads, and dessert, at this all-you-can-eat buffet. This easy, full-day tour combines a thrilling whale-watching cruise with a relaxing Alaska salmon bake for the best of picturesque Juneau!
highlights: Ply pristine Alaskan waters in a jet-powered boat specifically designed for them.
Indulge yourself in a satisfying buffet that focuses on Alaskan specialties. After lunch, return to the dock or stay for a marshmallow roast.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Endicott Arm Fjord and Glacier Explorer
rows.description: Your adventure begins when you board a high-speed expedition vessel directly from your cruise ship. Relax in the warm, spacious cabin, surrounded by large windows as your experienced captain guides the vessel past shimmering icebergs. The singular maneuverability of this vessel allows you to get as close as possible to the ever-changing face of the Dawes Glacier. From the topside observation deck, absorb this unforgettable experience through all your senses. You can sometimes even feel the "white thunder" as immense spires of ice break from the glacier and crash into the sea. While exploring the length of this ice-carved fjord, you will cruise beneath cliffs rising high above the jade-colored waters. As occasions arise, the captain will stop the vessel to allow for viewing and photography of wildlife, such as pigeon guillemots, bald eagles, harbor seals, mountain goats, and bears. Departing Endicott Arm, the onboard naturalist will provide a wealth of information about the area, which naturalist John Muir called a "wild, untamed Yosemite."
highlights: Live your Alaskan dreams on this up-close exploration of spectacular Endicott Arm, including exciting views of the impressive Dawes Glacier.
Weave through shimmering icebergs and travel near the rugged shoreline on a nimble vessel.
Listen as a knowledgeable naturalist and crew provide compelling narration about this wildly beautiful area.
Enjoy a variety of complimentary snacks and beverages served by your friendly Alaskan crew as you trace the rugged shores of Stephens Passage on the cruise north to Juneau.

rows.title: Small Group: Paddle with Whales Kayak Adventure
restrictions: Are you less than or equal to 275.0 lbs ?
Are you greater than or equal to 8.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 80.0 inches ?
rows.description: Immerse yourself in Alaska’s untouched wilderness on this Alaskan must-do experience. You'll board a small boat and venture up the Inside Passage to the richest humpback whale feeding grounds in Alaska. As the captain cruises through the Channel Island State Marine Park, your naturalist guide will provide a natural history narrative of this diverse ecosystem featuring Alaska's most majestic creatures. Your captain will land the boat on a remote island just in time for some hearty Alaskan snacks. Before launching the kayaks into the ocean, your kayak guide will provide safety and paddle instruction, and then you will embark on an intimate wildlife viewing quest. Keep your eyes on the water for breaching humpbacks, killer whales, porpoises and sea lions! Don't forget to also cast your eyes skyward, where bald eagles and other sea birds may be overlooking your journey, waiting to be spotted. At the conclusion of your kayaking adventure, you will cruise back to the mainland enjoying further wildlife viewing opportunities.
highlights: Travel to prime whale and wildlife viewing areas on board our 6-passenger motorboat
Explore remote waters and spot wildlife by kayak, accompanied by your guide.
Discover the natural history of the Inside Passage and revel in fantastic mountain and glacier views
Recharge with hearty snacks and a bento box lunch

rows.title: Small Group: Mendenhall Glacier by Helicopter and Dog Sledding
restrictions: Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Embark on a journey that takes to the air and sets you down on one of Alaska’s most famous glaciers. Begin by boarding a helicopter and taking off into the crisp Alaskan sky. During your flight you will have a bird’s-eye view of the lush Tongass National Rainforest, soaring mountain peaks, and the spectacular Mendenhall Glacier. After you touch down on the glacier, Step out and marvel at the frozen landscape. Meet up with a group of expert mushers, and 300 energetic Alaskan Huskies, to learn the ins and outs of the state’s favorite and furriest sport. Snap photos with the irresistible crowd of canines, and then experience the adventure of a lifetime. Just sit back and enjoy the ride.
highlights: Ride a helicopter over some of Juneau's most striking landscapes.
Feel the thrill of mushing across Mendenhall Glacier with your very own sled dog team.
Enjoy an exciting interactive experience with approximately 300 Alaskan Huskies.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: O Canada! Night Lights with Timbits
rows.description: Embark on an enchanting shore excursion through Victoria’s inner harbor, where the city’s twinkling lights create a mesmerizing urban landscape.
highlights: Board a comfortable motor coach and begin your tour at Victoria’s inner harbor, where the gentle glow of city lights reflects off the calm waters, creating a picturesque start to your journey.
Your first stop is at the iconic Canadian coffee chain, Tim Hortons, where you can warm up with a cup of coffee or hot chocolate and enjoy a selection of Timbits.
After, marvel at the majestic Fairmont Empress Hotel, illuminated against the night sky. The grand, historic building stands as a beacon of elegance and charm, captivating visitors with its beauty.

Next, admire the striking Legislative Assembly of British Columbia, outlined in brilliant white lights, which offer a stunning visual treat and highlight the building's architectural grandeur and historical significance.
Continue your drive past the Royal BC Museum and the Empress Hotel, both appearing even more dramatic and captivating under the night sky.

Cruise along the inner harbor, where the calm waters mirror the city lights, enhancing the magical ambiance of the evening. Enjoy panoramic views of Victoria’s illuminated urban landscape, showcasing the city’s beauty and charm.
As you conclude the “Oh Canada! Night Lights with Timbits” tour, receive a special Canadian goodie bag as a memento of your memorable evening excursion.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Malahat Skywalk and Victoria Highlights
rows.description: Ascend to new heights and see the towering beauty of British Columbia from the unique perspective of the Malahat Skywalk that invites guests to 360-degree views of the mountains, forests and islands surrounding Victoria.
highlights: Enjoy a scenic, narrated ride to one of B.C.'s foremost attractions, Malahat Skywalk, the dramatic, spiral structure that rises 800 feet above Vancouver Island and offers sweeping views of distant mountains, lush forests and pristine waters of the Salish Sea.
Ascend the skywalk's gradually inclining spiral ramps, with British Columbia's awesome natural beauty unveiled with every turn and pivot, including Mt. Baker, Finlayson Arm, the Saanich Peninsula.
Looking for a thrill? Literally walk across the sky on the skywalk's Adventure Net suspended across the center of the structure, then shoot down the 64-foot spiral slide for a delirious descent.

Back on the ground, reboard your coach for a guided, panoramic tour of Victoria, the "city of gardens," that introduces Old Town, the tranquil gardens and trails of Beacon Hill Park, the iconic Empress Hotel and the bustling Inner Harbour, before the return drive to meet your ship.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Waterfront and Wildlife Cruise
rows.description: Cruise along Ketchikan's local waterfront aboard an Alaskan-built, expedition-style vessel, as you learn about the region’s rich maritime history and explore the waters in search of wildlife.
highlights: Begin your immersive nature experience, as you hop aboard a deluxe, Alaskan-built expedition vessel (complete with comfortable seating, a heated cabin and wrap-around windows) and set sail across the beautiful harbors of Ketchikan's dynamic waterfront.

Soak in the breathtaking views of the rugged Alaskan wilderness, as you learn about the town’s rich maritime culture, along with its colorful past, including rum runners, fish pirates and so much more.

Experience Ketchikan from a unique vantage point — your boat! Take in stunning views of one of historic Creek Street and the world’s largest collection of totem poles at Saxman Native Village, all without ever stepping ashore.

Along the way, navigate the waters within the tribal boundaries of the Metlakatla Indian Community in search of local wildlife among the tidelines of Nichol's Passage, which if luck permits, may include curious whales, lounging seals, majestic bald eagles and many other species.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: A Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show
rows.description: Marvel as Ketchikan’s celebrated world-class timber athletes compete and entertain in the famous Ketchikan Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show. Watch a show heralded a “must-see” by the Travel Channel. Marvel as Alaskan history comes alive in Alaska’s rowdiest show. The cheering and heckles will become roars of excitement as two rugged teams of champion lumberjack athletes compete in chopping, sawing, tree climbing, log rolling, and axe throwing. See the Ketchikan lumberjacks compete in 12 different thrilling events. Enjoy music and incredible entertainment while learning about Alaskan logging history. The historic show is located on the waterfront shopping district just a few blocks from the port. Sit in comfortable heated, cushioned, and covered grandstand seats while watching this actual Alaskan competition. Have your camera ready for the incredible lumberjack action.
highlights: Watch Alaska's rich logging history come to life as you visit The Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show.
See the world's best timber athletes go head to head in more than a dozen exciting and dangerous events.

rows.description: Starting off with a scenic drive from Ketchikan to Clover Pass Resort, this combination Alaskan lodge and sea feast excursion delivers both the natural wonders and tastes of Alaska. From the resort, board an adventure boat in search of wildlife, visit an eagle's nest and venture past a US submarine testing site, before stopping at Silverking Lodge within the Tongass National Forest. rows.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Railway and Klondike Highway Journey
rows.description: Step back in time to relive the glory days of Alaska's Gold Rush as you board a vintage rail car and retrace the original route up to the Yukon's White Pass Summit. The world-famous narrow gauge White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad, revered as the "Scenic Railway of the World ", promises an unforgettable ride along the eastern side of the historic White Pass as you retrace the steps of early gold seekers, venturing along an iron trail that was built over 100 years ago through some of the North's most rugged terrain. Venture 20 miles toward the impressive 2,865-foot summit, enjoying views of Alaska's lush and verdant landscape along the way. Upon arrival at the summit, you'll board a motor coach for a narrated tour back to Skagway. Soak in the marvelous scenery and legendary landmarks as you pass by the historical areas of Brackett Wagon Road, Pitch Fork Falls, Dead Horse Gulch, and the infamous Tormented Valley. Throughout the journey, your guide will regale you with stories of the hardships faced by the stampeders, and how they overcame their struggles against the harsh climate and topography of the region.
highlights: Experience the beauty of the Yukon’s White Pass Summit by both train and motor coach.
Relive the history of Alaska’s Gold Rush era, following in the footsteps of the early gold seekers, as you ride the White Pass Railway and climb the same route as Gold Rush Stampeders.
Journey 20 miles from tidewater to the summit of the White Pass - a 2,865-foot elevation.

Learn about the significance of the White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad, built in 1898 during the Klondike Gold Rush and regarded a Historic Civil Engineering Landmark.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Glacier Gardens and Salmon Bake
rows.description: Take a fully narrated tour of Alaska's Glacier Gardens Rainforest and wrap up with an Alaskan-style salmon feast. Hear how local owners came to develop the area and the history behind their notorious upside down trees. Then, embark on a mile long journey into the rainforest. Enjoy a brief stop in the greenhouse at the bottom of the mountain before traveling to a lovely spot in the Alaskan rainforest for an al fresco salmon bake. Relax to musical entertainment and warm up in the covered and heated pavilion. Or, go for a trek to find a nearby creek, waterfall, and the remnants of an abandoned mine. Then, it’s time to savor a feast of alder wood-grilled salmon, barbecue ribs, and an assortment of sides sure to satisfy the heartiest wilderness appetite.
highlights: Ascend to the heart of the rainforest at an elevation of 580 feet, and soak in the breathtaking views.
Explore Rainforest Gardens' colorful history as you walk through its lower garden, amazingly designed landscapes, and cascading stream.
Savor an outdoor Alaskan salmon bake, featuring alder wood-grilled salmon and all the trimmings.

Enjoy a brief stop in the greenhouse at the bottom of the mountain before traveling to a lovely spot in the Alaskan rainforest for an al fresco salmon bake. Relax to musical entertainment and warm up in the covered and heated pavilion. Or, go for a trek to find a nearby creek, waterfall, and the remnants of an abandoned mine. Then, it’s time to savor a feast of alder wood-grilled salmon, barbecue ribs, and an assortment of sides sure to satisfy the heartiest wilderness appetite.

Ascend to the heart of the rainforest at an elevation of 580 feet, and soak in the breathtaking views.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Wildlife Safari and Beach Campfire
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 5.0 years ?
rows.description: Search for Alaska's wildlife aboard our expedition vessel, followed by a visit to a remote beach where you'll enjoy the warm hospitality of Native camp hosts. Meet your knowledgeable captain who will use his understanding of tides, weather, and seasonal variations to design a one-of-a-kind wildlife-viewing expedition. Keep on the lookout for humpback whales, orcas, Dall's porpoise, harbor seals, bears, and eagles. Learn about the life-cycles and behaviors of these animals from your onboard naturalist. Then, journey to the remote Annette Island where you'll meet your Tsimshian Alaska Native hosts. Explore the old growth rainforest and learn traditional harvesting techniques in the intertidal zone. After the fire has been lit ( "La Gwelga Lak ") you'll savor reindeer sausage, a variety of chips, salmonberry jam, and other local delights beside the mesmerizing campfire. Roast marshmallows for s’mores with chocolate. Hot cocoa, water, and coffee will also be available onboard.

Journey to the remote Annette Island where you’ll meet your Tsimshian Alaska Native camp hosts.
Learn intertidal harvesting techniques, while exploring a pristine Alaskan beach.
Savor delicious Alaskan flavors and dishes beside a crackling bonfire.
Enjoy unparalleled access to Alaska’s remote wilderness, while enjoying local culture and flavors on this intimate, boutique experience.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Rainforest Canoe and Nature Trail
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 40.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Today's adventure begins with a scenic drive to a secluded mountain lake, where a 37-foot Native American-style canoe - fast, stable and easy to maneuver - awaits. As you paddle under the direction of an experienced guide, scan the shore for wildlife and learn about the natural and native history of the area. The lake is surrounded by the pristine Tongass National Forest, the largest national forest in the United States and the world's largest temperate rainforest. It's the ideal place to paddle a canoe and let the stillness of nature envelope you. Step ashore at a remote base camp and enjoy an Alaska-style snack of homemade smoked salmon spread, clam chowder, rolls and wild-berry jam before following your knowledgeable guide on a short nature walk. The guide will point out the unusual flora and fauna of the old-growth forest, including a carnivorous plant, the sundew, that uses tentacles with adhesive "dew " to capture small insects. Don't miss this rare opportunity to comfortably explore Southeast Alaska the same way it was discovered by its original explorers, in harmony with nature and in tune with the haunting beauty of wild Alaska.
highlights: Fall under the spell of the peace, stillness and unparalleled splendor of Tongass National Forest as you paddle across a serene lake and walk among the towering trees.
Paddle a native canoe to experience these surroundings as pioneers did.
Learn about the plants and animals that inhibit this old-growth forest.

rows.title: Small Group: Alaska's Whales, Glaciers and Rainforest Walk
rows.description: Delve into the wonders of Juneau's wilderness as your professional interpretive guide leads you through the temperate rainforest surrounding the Mendenhall Glacier. Mendenhall, one of the most spectacular - yet accessible - glaciers in North America, is just 13 miles from downtown Juneau. A half-mile wide, it's also part of a 13-mile Juneau Ice Field, which terminates in Mendenhall Lake, where blue icebergs bob in the shadow of the stately Coast Mountains. The glacier is surrounded by trails of varying distances, from which you can gaze at salmon streams or marvel at tumbling waterfalls. Black bears, porcupines, beavers, and bald eagles inhabit the area around the trails, and sightings are likely. Then, take to the water in a vessel designed for marine viewing, with open stern and bow, large panel windows, and a low-to-the-water vantage point. The local captain and professional naturalist intimately know these waters and the humpback whales, orcas, and other sea creatures that inhabit them, and excel at spotting them while keeping a respectful distance. This tour unveils all the natural wonders that Juneau and the Alaskan wilderness have to offer, in a personable setting that engages both the seasoned and the novice traveler.
highlights: Gaze at the majestic Coast Mountains reflected in the calm waters of Mendenhall Lake.
Join your professional naturalist and seasoned captain as they share their local expertise and deepen your connection to Juneau.
Stretch your legs and expand your knowledge of Alaskan wildlife on a gentle hike around the Mendenhall Glacier recreational area, one of the continent's most accessible glaciers.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Musher's Camp and Sled Dog Experience
rows.description: Your adventure begins with a fascinating historic tour led by your knowledgeable guide through downtown Skagway and along nine miles of breathtaking coastline to our Musher’s Camp in Dyea. From there, you will embark on an exciting ride up a rugged mountain road in our specialized unimog, surrounded by stunning glacial streams and waterfalls. Upon reaching the top, you will be greeted by your musher and a team of energetic Alaskan Huskies, ready to take you on an exhilarating ride down a mile-long trail deep within the Tongass National Rainforest on a custom-designed, wheeled sled. After your thrilling ride, your unimog will take you to a breathtaking overlook of the Dyea tidal flats, surrounded by stunning scenery. Next, enjoy a "Kennel Talk," where we will share fascinating information about dog sledding and answer any questions you may have. You'll also have plenty of time to play with our adorable puppies, whose socialization is essential to their racing success. Finally, your visit will be completed with a cozy fire and a unique gift shop.
highlights: Enjoy an enchanting 1-mile dog sled ride through the forest.
See Alaska's awe-inspiring beauty with a private overlook of the beautiful Dyea Valley and Lynn Canal.
Meet the future generations and cuddle with adorable pups after your sledding adventure.
Take an exciting ride on our specialized 4X4 Unimog up the mountain to the dog sled training area

rows.title: Small Group: Alaskan Halibut Fishing
restrictions: Are you less than or equal to 350.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Begin your Alaskan fishing adventure on a scenic 30-minute cruise with sight possibilities of glaciers, whales, and other marine mammals. Your target fish will be halibut; however, you might also catch other bottom fish, such as pacific cod and a variety of rockfish. Fish in the local hot spots for approximately three hours as you enjoy snacks, refreshing beverages, and spectacular views. Then, have your catch professionally processed and shipped home.
highlights: Enjoy a scenic cruise on route to your fishing grounds and spot a variety of marine mammals along the way.
Explore the abundant waters of Juneau, Alaska and fish for halibut, cod, and more.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Discover the Mendenhall Glacier
rows.description: Behold the rugged beauty of the Alaskan wilderness with an intrepid shore excursion to the Mendenhall Glacier, where you embark on a self-guided exploration of the region and experience the awe-inspiring grandeur of this icy giant.
highlights: Enjoy a narrated motor coach drive along the Gastineau Channel to one of Juneau's most incredible landmarks - the Mendenhall Glacier, a magnificent 13-mile expanse of shimmering blue ice. Along the way, pass by the infamous Gold Creek and listen to your knowledgeable driver talk about the history of the capital.

Once you reach the glacier, enjoy free time to soak in the beauty of this natural wonder; walk along the trails and keep your camera at the ready to capture unobstructed views of the river of ice. Make sure to stay vigilant for any local wildlife sightings, including porcupines, bears, Arctic Terns, beavers and mountain goats.

You can also explore the visitor’s center, which sits along a cliff over the valley floor and offers a number of interpretive exhibits related to the glacier's history. Browse the gift shop for a souvenir to bring back home, before another scenic drive brings your Mendenhall Glacier excursion to a conclusion.

rows.title: Small Group: Fly-in Norris Glacier Hike and Packraft
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 10.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
Are you less than or equal to 6.0 feet ?
rows.description: Adventure seekers, fly to Norris Glacier to packraft in the frigid waters of the Last Frontier before strapping on your micro-spikes for some glacier hiking as you explore this uniquely gorgeous site's mesmerizing, moonscape-like surface.
highlights: Board a six-passenger floatplane and soar through the skies of Alaska, enjoying awe-inspiring views as you glide toward the mouth of Taku Inlet, the gateway to the largest flowing glaciers in the Juneau Icefield.
Touch down on a glacial lake flanked by two magnificent glaciers, Norris and Taku, and begin your guided hike to "Packraft Cove" as you learn about packrafting's unique and thrilling charms.

Hop into a one-person packraft, an inflatable boat light that allows for deft navigation, and take to the waters, paddling past icebergs and immersing yourself in the pristine surroundings as you make your way towards the terminus of the glacier.
Trade your paddles for micro-spike crampons and a trekking pole to hike Norris Glacier and experience the unique sensation of walking on glacial ice.
After completing the glacier hike, paddle back to the cove, enjoying one final glimpse of the Alaska wild before boarding the floatplane to fly back to Juneau.

rows.title: Small Group: Chilkoot Canoe Wildlife Safari
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 6.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 300.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Glide into the rugged natural beauty of Alaska with this immersive shore excursion, as you hop into a canoe and set off from the town of Haines in search of the perfect blend of history, wildlife, and stunning landscapes.
highlights: Your day begins at the port in Skagway, where you enjoy a memorable ferry ride to Haines. Along the way, a knowledgeable onboard naturalist points out wildlife and discusses the geography, history and people of Southeast Alaska.

Upon your arrival in Haines, board a bus for a brief exploration of this charming town. Known as the "Adventure Capital of Alaska," Haines is steeped in history and surrounded by stunning natural beauty.

After your scenic drive along Lutak Inlet and into the river valley, arrive at tranquil Chilkoot Lake, framed by majestic mountains and dense forests. Following a safety briefing and some paddling instruction, gear up with a personal flotation device and board a Voyageur canoe, modeled after those used by gold miners and trappers in the late 1800s.
Set out across the serene waters, either paddling or letting the motor assist, as you explore the lake’s hidden inlets, coves, and salmon spawning streams.

After snapping a few post-worthy pictures, return to Haines for a relaxing picnic-style lunch at a scenic oceanfront location. Savor a selection of fresh, local fare and admire the panoramic views of the surrounding mountains, before another ferry ride brings your Chilkoot Canoe Wildlife Safari excursion to a conclusion.

rows.title: Whispers of Ketchikan: A Burlesque Journey Through History
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 21.0 years ?
rows.description: Experience the intoxicating charm of Ketchikan’s Creek Street – once the beating heart of the town’s red-light district and now a waterfront stage for an unforgettable shore excursion, featuring a captivating burlesque experience.
highlights: Your burlesque journey through history begins with a stroll along Creek Street, your guide painting vivid scenes of the prohibition-era heyday, when the district bustled with dance halls, speakeasies, and legendary personalities.
Inside an intimate local venue, the lights dim, the band strikes its first note, and the ‘Whispers of Ketchikan’ cast takes the stage.

The show unfolds in a series of captivating acts—from hypnotic fan dances and skillful hula hoop artistry to juggling and sultry, slow-burn performances—all woven seamlessly with tales of the women who made Creek Street famous.

As the final number brings the crowd to cheers, leave with a new appreciation for Ketchikan’s colorful history and a night of entertainment that’s as bold and unforgettable as the women who inspired it.

Once inside the state-of-the-art theater, the adventure launches into "Edge of the Wild," a 20-minute cinematic journey designed to thrill all your senses.
As you buckle in, prepare to soar above ancient glaciers, plunge into roaring whitewater rapids, and sweep across the rugged wilderness teeming with wildlife.
Thanks to cutting-edge 4D effects, you can feel the wind in your face, the mist of waterfalls on your skin, and even the chill of falling snow — all while comfortably seated.
After the curtains close on your Alaska: Edge of the Wild excursion, walk away with an unforgettable impression of Alaska’s raw beauty and adventurous spirit.

rows.title: Space Needle, Pike Place Market & City Tour-Airport
restrictions: Are you between 1.0 and 99.0 years ?
rows.description: The “Emerald City” shines with energy, culture, and spectacular scenery — and this post-cruise shore excursion is the perfect way to experience its highlights before you head home.
highlights: Your journey begins as you board a comfortable motor coach with your knowledgeable local guide, who brings the city’s past and present to life through engaging stories and fun facts.
The tour kicks off with a drive through downtown Seattle, where modern glass towers rise alongside the city’s bustling sports stadiums, home to the Mariners, Seahawks, and Sounders.

The route continues through the International District, also known as Seattle’s Chinatown, a vibrant neighborhood where the aromas of dim sum and pho mingle with the sounds of traditional music and the chatter of locals.

Next, step back in time in Pioneer Square, the birthplace of modern Seattle. After the Great Fire of 1889, the area was rebuilt with stately brick architecture that still stands today, making it a national historic district.
The tour then takes you to Seattle’s most famous landmark – the Space Needle. Built for the 1962 World’s Fair, this futuristic structure has become an enduring symbol of innovation.
In just 43 seconds, a high-speed elevator whisks you 520 feet into the sky to the observation deck, where you’re greeted by a 360-degree panorama of the city.
Back at street level, time to dive into Seattle’s heart and soul — the Pike Place Market.

Open since 1907, this vibrant, nine-acre public market buzzes with life. Watch fishmongers hurl salmon through the air in their famous fish toss, browse artisan stalls filled with handmade crafts and local produce, and sample fresh seafood, flowers, and pastries.
Don’t miss a stop at the original Starbucks, where you can sip a cappuccino and take in the birthplace of the global coffee culture.

Enjoy some free time to wander, shop, and soak up the market’s energy, before heading to Seattle-Tacoma International Airport and concluding your Space Needle, Pike Palace Market, and City Tour excursion.
rows.copyForAnalysis:
Space Needle, Pike Place Market & City Tour-Airport								

The “Emerald City” shines with energy, culture, and spectacular scenery — and this post-cruise shore excursion is the perfect way to experience its highlights before you head home.

Your journey begins as you board a comfortable motor coach with your knowledgeable local guide, who brings the city’s past and present to life through engaging stories and fun facts.

The tour kicks off with a drive through downtown Seattle, where modern glass towers rise alongside the city’s bustling sports stadiums, home to the Mariners, Seahawks, and Sounders.

The route continues through the International District, also known as Seattle’s Chinatown, a vibrant neighborhood where the aromas of dim sum and pho mingle with the sounds of traditional music and the chatter of locals.

Next, step back in time in Pioneer Square, the birthplace of modern Seattle. After the Great Fire of 1889, the area was rebuilt with stately brick architecture that still stands today, making it a national historic district.

The tour then takes you to Seattle’s most famous landmark – the Space Needle. Built for the 1962 World’s Fair, this futuristic structure has become an enduring symbol of innovation.

In just 43 seconds, a high-speed elevator whisks you 520 feet into the sky to the observation deck, where you’re greeted by a 360-degree panorama of the city.

Back at street level, time to dive into Seattle’s heart and soul — the Pike Place Market.

Open since 1907, this vibrant, nine-acre public market buzzes with life. Watch fishmongers hurl salmon through the air in their famous fish toss, browse artisan stalls filled with handmade crafts and local produce, and sample fresh seafood, flowers, and pastries.

Don’t miss a stop at the original Starbucks, where you can sip a cappuccino and take in the birthplace of the global coffee culture.

Enjoy some free time to wander, shop, and soak up the market’s energy, before heading to Seattle-Tacoma International Airport and concluding your Space Needle, Pike Palace Market, and City Tour excursion.

rows.title: Wilderness Zodiac Quest
restrictions: Are you between 6.0 and 75.0 years ?
Are you between 40.0 and 300.0 lbs ?
Are you between 42.0 and 75.0 inches ?
rows.description: Feel the rush of freedom on this unforgettable Alaskan shore excursion, as you pilot your own Zodiac boat through the rugged beauty of the George Inlet Fjords, surrounded by towering cliffs, emerald forests, and pristine waters alive with wildlife.
highlights: Your journey begins with a guided tour through historic downtown Ketchikan, where your driver points out the city’s highlights — colorful totem poles, the artistry of Saxman Native Village, and the shimmering waters of the Tongass Narrows.
Your scenic drive winds along the coastline to Beaver Falls Salmon Cannery, where the crisp rainforest air welcomes you to the heart of wild Alaska.
From here, take a short walk through a lush, moss-draped trail leading to the Zodiac basecamp.

After a safety orientation and gear fitting, suit up in rain gear, rubber boots, and a life jacket before stepping aboard your own Zodiac — a nimble, four- or six-passenger inflatable boat built for speed and maneuverability.
Following your expert guide, cruise through the stunning George Inlet Fjords, where waterfalls, dense forests, and rocky shorelines tell the story of Alaska’s untamed beauty.

After a thrilling experience on the water, return to the cannery for well-earned snacks and refreshments, reflecting on your Wilderness Zodiac Quest excursion and the wonders of Ketchikan’s wild coast.

rows.title: Small Group: Urban Hike and Brew
rows.description: Lace up your favorite walking shoes and embark on a journey through Alaska’s so-called “first city,” as you discover the stories, monuments and pieces of history that earned Ketchikan its reputation as one of the wickedest towns in the West.
highlights: Meet with your knowledgeable guide and begin your immersive journey along Ketchikan’s steep hills and side streets, as you learn about the city’s transformation from fishing village to seedy frontier town to scenic modern port.

Wander the city’s wooden walkways and discover a number of historic/scenic highlights, including Eagle Park and its iconic carved bird of prey, the infamous former red light district of Creek Street, City Park and the salmon spawning stream of Ketchikan Creek.
Watch for salmon and salmon seekers - both human and animal - as you stroll along the creek to its iconic salmon ladder, where thousands of fish work their way vertically up the current.

Make sure to keep an eye out for Ketchikan’s iconic totem poles, including the 55-foot-tall Chief Johnson totem pole, which was carved from a single red cedar log and depicts the Tlinglit tribe’s legends of Fog Woman and Raven.
After, stop by a nearby pub, where you can enjoy a complimentary glass of locally brewed beer before returning to your awaiting ship."

rows.title: Small Group: Alaska Bear Adventure by Floatplane
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 8.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Take off by floatplane as your pilot guides you to a remote site in the Tongass National Forest noted for its salmon rich streams and abundant wildlife. A 20 minute flight takes you by historic homesteads and over the sparkling waters of Alaska’s Inside Passage. Then a short hike with an Alaskan naturalist leads you to your viewing platform overlooking a Salmon spawning stream. Look down to see schools of salmon, Black bears and other wildlife.
highlights: Look down from your viewing platform to see large numbers of salmon congregate to complete their spawning journey.
Travel by floatplane, viewing a landscape of forested mountains and pristine lakes on your way to the viewing location.
See Black Bears feed on Salmon while viewing a variety of birds, Sitka Black-tailed deer, mink and Marten.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Backcountry Jeep and Canoe Safari
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 5.0 years ?
Are you greater than or equal to 40.0 lbs ?
rows.description: You'll begin your Jeep portion of the tour by heading into the mountainous roads of Alaska's Tongass National Forest. You'll be fascinated by the area's natural history as you pass through old and new growth forests while your guide provides informative commentary via 2-way radio. You'll navigate through old logging roads to Lake Harriet Hunt where you'll paddle in a 37-foot, 20 passenger, native-style canoe across a mountain lake where a hearty snack awaits. KE06
highlights: Fulfill your sense of adventure as you wind a Jeep Wrangler® through the rugged back roads of Revillagigedo Island.
Experience the unique ecosystem of Lake Harriet Hunt, including the Sundew, a rare carnivorous plant, by paddling a guided native-style canoe through its waters.
Enjoy a short nature walk through an old-growth forest on the other side of the lake and taste a hearty Alaskan snack prepared for you by the camp cook.

rows.title: Small Group: Juneau Salmon Fishing Adventure
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 5.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 350.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Beat the bears to the punch for that dreamed-of trophy salmon, by getting out on the protected inside waterways for a day of sportfishing. Enjoy rod and reel trolling in one of the best fishing grounds in southeast Alaska from the comfort of a charter boat skippered by a U.S. Coast Guard-licensed captain. Additionally, for an extra fee, your fish may be processed and shipped home. (U.S. addresses only).
highlights: Troll one of the most abundant fishing grounds in Southeast Alaska from the comfort of your charter vessel.
Catch fresh fish in protected inside waters, while marveling at the stunning scenery surrounding you.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: Zodiac Whale Watching & Wildlife Tour
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 6.0 years ?
Are you between 50.0 and 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Hop aboard our high-speed zodiac for a thrilling wildlife-viewing adventure along Victoria's breathtaking west coast. Victoria is considered the world's best location to view whales in the wild. Our Rigid Hull Inflatable Boats bring you closer to the ocean and to the action, immersing you in the magnificent beauty of the Pacific Ocean. Join your skilled captain in search of whales as you zoom through the beautiful waters of the Juan de Fuca and Haro Strait, renowned for their incredible wildlife. Keep on the lookout for transient orcas, humpback whales, Minke whales, seals, sea lions, and marine birds. As you race across the sea, you'll be closer to the elements, giving you a once-in-a-lifetime view of these stunning animals. Our eco-friendly zodiacs are celebrated for their ultra-low emissions, quiet operation, stability, and comfort. Experience an adventure recognized by Surfrider, TripAdvisor, and the Vancouver Island Sustainability Award.
highlights: Marvel at one of the world's most breathtaking coastlines on this thrilling zodiac adventure.
Learn about Canada's stunning west coast wildlife, looking for orcas, humpback whales, seals, sea lions, and birds.
Experience the best location in the world to view whales in the wild.
Race across the sea in search of the wildlife in Juan de Fuca and Haro Strait.

rows.title: Small Group: Pilot's Choice Glacier Exploration by Helicopter
restrictions: Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Enjoy an eagle's-eye view by helicopter of high alpine lakes, "icefalls" hanging from sheer cliffs, and some of the most remote peaks and glaciers in the Juneau Icefield. Then, your pilot will land in two different locations, where you'll continue your tour on foot to truly appreciate the beauty of this ancient landscape. With your pilot as your guide enjoy 30-minutes of exploration time between these two landings to discover the moraines, crevasses, seracs, and glaciers up close.
highlights: Enjoy a 50-minute flight over Juneau Icefield past Wonderland, Glacier King, the Mendenhall Towers, and Devil's Paw.
Trek on foot after landing in two locations and witness firsthand a variety of geological and environmental processes at work.
