rows.title: Small Group: White Pass Legacy Club Luxury Railway Experience
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 16.0 years ?
rows.description: Experience the dramatic beauty of Southeast Alaska in opulence while traveling aboard the White Pass & Yukon Routes' luxurious Legacy Club Car with your very own private guide. The world-famous narrow gauge White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad, revered as the “Scenic Railway of the World”, promises an unforgettable ride along the eastern side of the historic White Pass. And today, you’ll enjoy the exclusive opportunity to ascend over 2,800 feet from sea to Summit in style and comfort while aboard the Legacy Club Car. Retrace the steps of Alaska’s gold seekers as you venture along the centuries-old trail in your luxury car, relishing the custom-made leather seating, all-inclusive beverages, and regionally-sourced appetizers. Throughout your journey, your private guide will highlight all the scenic viewpoints and regale you with the incredible story of the railroad’s beginnings. The White Pass Legacy Club is the ideal way to discover legendary White Pass, with a modern luxury twist.
highlights: Sit back and relax as you travel across Southeast Alaska in your luxury parlor car, enjoying delicious appetizers and all-inclusive beverages along the way.
Join an intimate group of travelers for an exclusive rail experience aboard the legendary White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad’s luxurious Legacy Club Car, complete with your own private guide.

Soak in spectacular views of Alaska’s iconic wilderness as you ascend to the White Pass Summit, passing by breathtaking mountains, gorges, waterfalls, tunnels, trestles, and historic sites throughout your journey.
Learn about the impressive history of the railway, built in 1898 during the Klondike Gold Rush and regarded a Historic Civil Engineering Landmark.

rows.title: Small Group: White Pass Train and Klondike Bike Tour
restrictions: Are you less than or equal to 300.0 lbs ?
Are you greater than or equal to 54.0 inches ?
Are you greater than or equal to 13.0 years ?
rows.description: Experience the natural beauty of Skagway on the White Pass & Yukon Route Railway followed by a scenic bike ride. First, wind through Alaska’s magnificent coastal mountains on the way to Fraser station. Then, enjoy a narrated drive to the starting point of your bike ride, White Pass Summit. From here, we’ll ride as a group on an almost entirely downhill route back to Skagway. We stop to appreciate the majestic glaciers, mountains, and other historic sites along the way.
highlights: Board the White Pass and Yukon Route Railroad for a scenic mountain ride to British Columbia's Fraser Station.
Gear up and enjoy a scenic 15-mile bike ride downhill from White Pass Summit.
Discover waterfalls and glaciers along the White Pass and Yukon Railroad and while biking through Alaska's breathtaking mountains.

restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 10.0 years ?
Are you greater than or equal to 48.0 inches ?
Are you less than or equal to 350.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Board a US Coast Guard certified open-vessel for an unforgettable high-speed boat adventure in Lynn Canal, North America’s deepest and longest fjord. Outfitted with an exposure suit, you’ll stay warm and dry as you glide across the water and explore hidden coves and waterfalls. Immerse yourself in the wondrous sights of Alaska’s wilderness, while keeping an eye out for local wildlife as you hover close enough to feel the mist of the glacial waterfalls.
highlights: Enjoy a rafting excursion through Lynn Canal, offering an up-close and personal look of Alaska's majestic glacier waterfalls.
Experience possible sightings of Alaskan wildlife such as whales, eagles, seals, mountain goats, and more.

rows.title: Small Group: Interactive Self-Guided Jeep Tour 4-Door
rows.description: Embark on a self-guided journey through Juneau's stunning landscapes, all from the comfort and freedom of your own Jeep, as you capture the awe-inspiring beauty of Mendenhall Glacier or savor the tranquility of Eagle Beach.
highlights: Indulge in the freedom of your own four-door Jeep as you venture to Mendenhall Glacier, a magnificent 13-mile expanse of shimmering blue ice nestled against the backdrop of rugged mountains.
Marvel at the glacier's grandeur and witness its dynamic beauty, as it flows into Mendenhall Lake, creating a mesmerizing spectacle of nature's power and grace.
After, embark on a picturesque drive along the Alaska coast to reach the serene Shrine of St. Therese, nestled on a small island surrounded by pristine wilderness.
Discover the tranquility of this sacred site, where a quaint cobblestone church stands amidst towering trees, and keep an eye out for majestic humpback whales feeding in the surrounding waters.

Pause at Eagle Beach, a tranquil oasis where you can unwind at a scenic picnic table and take in the panoramic views of the Chilkat Mountains, while the gentle waters of the Eagle River meander beside you - the perfect backdrop for relaxation and reflection.
Next, traverse the scenic roads of Douglas Island, where breathtaking vistas of Mendenhall Glacier unfold before you, before making your way back to port.

Based on weather and seasonal factors, your guide will choose a fourth location that might include Douglas Island, DiPAC or one of several Juneau highlights. Guests are welcome to choose their own itinerary that the guide will program prior to their departure.

rows.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Railway and Skagway Highlights
rows.description: Return to the rough-and-tumble years of the Klondike Gold Rush and ride through breathtaking scenery on this panoramic excursion. You’ll begin by boarding a vintage railcar of the famed White Pass and Yukon Route Railway, a narrow-guide railroad that once carried gold seekers hoping to strike it rich. Settle into a comfy seat, a modern new addition, and rumble through Alaska’s evergreen forests in the shadows of soaring snow-capped mountains. Marvel at the unspoiled vistas on your outbound journey that crosses over the Canadian border. Transfer to a motor coach for a return trip that showcases natural wonders and remnants of the Gold Rush era. Stop at the Historic Gold Rush Cemetery on your return to hear stories of the past and learn about the colorful characters buried there. You’ll also pass Dead Horse Gulch, where nearly 3,000 overloaded and overworked pack animals died as fevered stampeders rushed forth to stake their claims.
highlights: Take an unforgettable train-ride along the eastern side of the White Pass.
Enjoy Alaskan Gold Rush stories as you follow in their footsteps on their original route.
Spend some leisure time exploring the historic town of Skagway before heading back to the ship.
Stop at the Historic Gold Rush Cemetery on your return to hear stories of the past and learn about the colorful characters buried there.

rows.title: Small Group: Norris Glacier by Helicopter and Dog Sledding
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 2.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 250.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Take to the skies above Juneau aboard a helicopter and fly off to a dog sledding experience on the Norris Glacier. Soar above the Juneau Icefield and enjoy a bird’s-eye view of historic gold mines, alpine lakes, and even your own cruise ship. After a bit of flightseeing, zoom off to the Norris Glacier. Marvel as your pilot touches down on the snowy surface and step out onto an otherworldly landscape. restrictions: Are you between 8.0 and 99.0 years ?
Are you less than or equal to 350.0 lbs ?
rows.description: Whether you're an experienced angler or a first-time fisherman, this shore excursion offers an unforgettable opportunity to reel in the legendary Alaskan Salmon, while enjoying first-class service, gourmet snacks, and stunning views of Alaska’s rugged wilderness.

Once you’ve made your catch, you have the option to have it professionally processed, portioned, frozen, and even smoked in true Alaskan style, before being shipped directly to your home.

rows.title: Mahoney Lake UTV Offroad Safari
restrictions: Are you between 6.0 and 99.0 years ?
Are you between 50.0 and 300.0 lbs ?
Are you between 40.0 and 75.0 inches ?
rows.description: Get ready to rev your engine and experience Alaska like never before on this thrilling off-road UTV shore excursion through the rugged wilderness of the Tongass National Forest - America’s largest temperate rainforest.
highlights: Your journey begins at port, where private transportation takes you along a scenic route toward the White River base camp.
Upon arrival, meet your UTV leader, gear up, and receive a quick orientation before hopping into your off-road vehicle.

With seatbelts fastened and cameras ready, set off in single-file formation, following your guide through five miles of rugged mountain trails that wind through the George Inlet Fjords and into the heart of the Tongass.
At the end of the trail lies Mahoney Lake, a secluded mountain gem fed by the cascading waters of Mahoney Falls.

After exploring and taking in the peaceful wilderness, time to refuel with a hearty outdoor lunch back at base camp, featuring a hot smoked reindeer link on a bun or a steaming cup of chili - paired with coffee, tea, or water.
Satisfied and smiling, re-board your vehicle for the scenic return to your ship—your heart still racing from your unforgettable Mahoney Lake UTV Offroad Safari excursion.

rows.title: Underground Gold Mine and Panning Tour
rows.description: Journey deep into Juneau’s mining history on this immersive shore excursion through the Alaska-Juneau Gold Mine, once part of the most productive gold-milling operation on Earth.
highlights: Your tour begins with a narrated drive from port along the picturesque Gastineau Channel, setting the scene for the mining story ahead.

Upon arrival at the Alaska Juneau Mine, gear up in a hard hat before following your experienced guide 360 feet into the mountain, where cool air and dim tunnels instantly transport you back to the early 1900s.
Inside the mine, learn about hard-rock mining techniques, see historic equipment come to life, and hear how miners labored in these rugged underground passages.
Above ground, explore the atmospheric ruins of the Gastineau Mill—opened in 1915 and once the world’s largest producing gold mill—and view the first steam locomotive ever brought to Alaska.

Feel the thunder of the John Peterson Stamp Mill, as it demonstrates how ore was crushed during the gold-mining boom. Then, try your hand at gold panning, with guaranteed “pay dirt” and the thrill of keeping any gold you uncover.

Before ending your Underground Gold Mine and Panning Tour excursion, browse historic relics and antiques displayed throughout the site, enjoy a complimentary hot beverage, and visit the General Store for unique souvenirs that honor Juneau’s golden legacy."

rows.title: Alaskan Sourdough History & Brick Oven Pizza Making
restrictions: Are you between 1.0 and 99.0 years ?
rows.description: From learning the age-old tradition of crafting sourdough starter to topping your own personal pizza, this shore excursion celebrates the region’s flavors in the most interactive way possible.
highlights: Your journey begins as you arrive at Eagles Nest and are welcomed into the warm, wood-fired aroma of the brick oven kitchen.

Your guide and chef shares the fascinating history of sourdough in Alaska—from Gold Rush-era miners who relied on their cherished starters to today’s tradition of transforming that tangy dough into perfect pizza crust.

After learning how sourdough starter becomes pizza dough, roll up your sleeves and create your own personal masterpiece, choosing from familiar favorites like pepperoni, mozzarella, sausage, and peppers, or diving into local specialties, such as smoked salmon, lemon ricotta, hot honey, pickled red onions, and house-made meatballs.

Once your pizza is baked to bubbling perfection, you settle onto the balcony with a locally brewed beer, a glass of wine, or a refreshing soda, taking in lively views of historic downtown Ketchikan and its colorful wooden storefronts.
Be sure to indulge in the Cape Fox Cannoli for dessert—a crisp pastry filled with sweet cream and chocolate chips that perfectly completes the experience.

After your meal, choose to end your Alaskan Sourdough History and Brick Oven Pizza Making excursion with a convenient ride back to port, or enjoy a leisurely walk to charming Creek Street, where boardwalks, galleries, and local history await your exploration.

rows.title: Canadian Whiskey & Chocolate Tasting
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 19.0 years ?
rows.description: Experience the perfect mix of Victoria charm and flavor with this captivating shore excursion, as you visit an award-winning distillery for a delectable whisky and chocolate tasting, followed by a narrated drive past the city’s most important highlights.
highlights: Hop aboard your luxury van and embark on a scenic drive through Victoria’s picturesque streets on your way to Macaloney’s Distillery and Brewery — an internationally acclaimed, Scottish-style craft distillery.

Here, enjoy an immersive tour of the distillery and learn about the single malt production process, from grain to glass, before savoring an award-winning whisky tasting, featuring local handcrafted chocolates from one of Victoria’s premier chocolatiers.

Your palate satisfied, conclude your Canadian Whiskey and Chocolate Tasting excursion with another scenic drive back to the ship, highlighting the charm of Victoria’s Inner Harbour and Chinatown - North America’s oldest.

rows.title: Alaskan Crab Brunch with Mimosa Flight
rows.description: Tantalize your taste buds (and your sense of exploration) with this flavorful Ketchikan shore excursion, as you visit a historical lodge for a delicious crab brunch, featuring local recipes and a flight of mimosas.
highlights: Your Alaskan journey begins with a scenic drive, passing by an old sawmill, majestic totem poles, and cascading waterfalls on your way to the George Inlet Lodge, a charming retreat that once served as a cannery bunkhouse in the early 1900s.

Here, prepare for a mouthwatering brunch, featuring a delicious crab casserole and half an Alaskan crab, accompanied by reindeer sausage, blueberry scones, smoked salmon dip, and a variety flight of three mimosas.

Admire the stunning waterfront views, before concluding your Crab Brunch Experience excursion with another scenic ride back to the pier, soaking in Alaska’s natural beauty one last time before returning to your ship.

rows.title: Old Town Axe Throwing & Chinatown Pinball
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 10.0 years ?
rows.description: It’s all fun and games (and a twist of local flavor) with this immersive Victoria shore excursion, as you spend the afternoon experiencing a couple of the city’s most exciting highlights, including axe throwing, pinball playing, and Chinatown exploring.
highlights: A narrated drive through Victoria brings you to a private venue in Old Town, where you can enjoy a bit of axe-throwing fun – just remember to aim at the target!

After, embark on a leisurely stroll through Victoria’s vibrant Chinatown, as you enjoy free time to explore; experience the thrill of pinball at Pinhalla Pizzeria, browse the local shops for a unique souvenir, or wander the historic Fan Tan Alley – North America’s narrowest commercial street.
Either way, conclude your Old Town Axe Throwing and Chinatown Pinball excursion with another scenic drive back to port, passing by more downtown highlights along the way.

rows.description: Step back in time with this captivating Victoria shore excursion, as you explore an iconic 19th-century castle, followed by a traditional Scottish tea experience at a beautifully restored mansion.
highlights: Your Victoria immersion begins with a scenic drive along the coastline on your way to Craigdarroch Castle, a Victorian-era Scottish baronial mansion and now a National Historic Site. Here, take the time to embark on a self-guided tour through this impressive mansion, as you admire the centuries of history and grandeur.

After, ride through Victoria’s historic Chinatown before arriving at Pendray Mansion for a traditional Scottish tea experience. Indulge your taste buds with a selection of savory regional treats, including smoked salmon blini, Welsh rarebit, cheddar scones, and more.
Your appetite satisfied, conclude your Craigdarroch Castle and Pendray Scottish Tea excursion with another drive back to port and your awaiting ship.

rows.title: Ghosts & Legends of Juneau
restrictions: Are you greater than or equal to 10.0 years ?
rows.description: Delve into Juneau’s supernatural past with this (allegedly) haunted shore excursion, as you embark on a guided stroll along the wooden boardwalks of Alaska’s capital, where eerie tales of centuries past blend together with scenic coastal charm.
highlights: Your foray into the supernatural begins in historic Juneau, where your guide leads you through its atmospheric downtown district.
As you walk along the charming streets and weathered boardwalks, listen to tales of ghostly apparitions and unexplained encounters, dating back to the city’s Gold Rush era.

At the historic Alaskan Hotel and Bar, the state’s oldest operating hotel, learn of the spectral “fallen woman” said to linger in its halls and the tragic sailor who leapt from a third-floor window - his presence reportedly still felt by guests.
Continue to the legendary Red Dog Saloon, a rowdy relic of frontier days where echoes of miners’ laughter and lingering spirits seem to blend.
Pass by Juneau Drug Company and the historic Silverbow Inn, each with its own whispered stories of unexplained footsteps and shadowy figures.

Along the Totem Pole Trail, your guide decodes Indigenous teachings and symbolism, sharing lore of shape-shifters, sea serpents, Thunderbirds, the elusive Hairy Man, and the mysterious Little People woven into Tlingit tradition.
The tales extend beyond town to the doomed miners of Treadwell Mine’s “Glory Hole” and the lonely beacon of Eldred Rock Lighthouse, where hapless sailors are said to haunt the icy waters.
As you conclude your Ghosts and Legends of Juneau excursion, return to port with a few spooky tales to retell and hopefully no unwanted ghouls following you back home.
